3.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Uncommon (green) quality Bind on Equip items that drop from enemies in the level 15-25 range. The primary source for obtaining these pieces is the Auction House. For self-farming, the items are world drops found most frequently in Classic dungeons with high mob density, such as The Deadmines, Wailing Caverns, and Shadowfang Keep. All main slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are part of this world drop loot table and are not tied to specific bosses. Any mob within the appropriate level range, both inside and outside dungeons, has a small chance to drop a piece.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as it consists of world drops. When farming in dungeons, you are subject to the retail limit of 10 instances per hour, per account. The entire set can be easily soloed by a current max-level character. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drops, a direct farm could take hundreds of dungeon resets or thousands of mob kills to complete. There is no tier token system for this set, and all pieces come from the same random drop source pool.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by searching for the "Sorcerer's" set pieces on the Auction House, as direct farming is unreliable. If you choose to farm it yourself, use a max-level character with high movement speed and area-of-effect abilities to clear low-level dungeons quickly. The Deadmines is a strong choice due to its linear layout and large number of non-boss enemies. Because all items are Bind on Equip, any duplicates you find can be sold on the Auction House to fund your search.

4.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon-quality world drops from zones and dungeons with level 20-30 mobs. Dungeons such as Razorfen Kraul, Shadowfang Keep, and Blackfathom Deeps are concentrated sources due to high mob density. Key zones for open-world farming include Ashenvale, Duskwood, and the Stonetalon Mountains. The item slots are Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist, all of which are obtained as random drops from mobs and chests within this level range. There are no specific bosses that drop these items; they are part of the global loot table for that level bracket.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming these items in the open world. If farming dungeons, normal difficulty instances can be entered and reset up to 10 times per hour, per account. The entire set can be easily soloed by any character at the current maximum level. The number of runs required is highly variable due to the random nature of world drops; it can take anywhere from a few hours to many farming sessions to collect all eight pieces. No tier tokens are associated with this set, and all pieces come from the same random drop source.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind on Equip (BoE), so the most efficient way to acquire them is often by searching for their appearances on the Auction House. If farming manually, use a high-mobility class with instant area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear mobs quickly. When farming dungeons, pull as many enemies as possible before using an AoE loot ability to maximize efficiency. There are no skips or shortcuts recommended, as the goal is to defeat the maximum number of mobs per hour.

6.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic ensemble is purchased from a vendor during the Trial of Style micro-holiday event. The event vendors are located near the transmogrifiers in major cities, such as Valdrakken, Orgrimmar, and Stormwind, but only while the event is active. The "Ensemble: Simple Regalia" is sold for 60 Trial of Style Tokens. This currency is exclusively earned by participating in the Trial of Style scenario, which players can queue for by speaking to an event NPC.

### Farming Logistics
The Trial of Style scenario has no lockout, allowing you to queue for it repeatedly while the micro-holiday is active. This is a solo-queued activity that places you in a 6-player competitive scenario, not a cooperative one. Earning the required 60 Trial of Style Tokens is fast, typically taking only two to four runs, as a first-place victory awards 50 tokens. Even lower placements grant a substantial number of tokens, ensuring a quick farm. All pieces are contained within the single purchased ensemble, so there are no other sources to track. The event itself is the only time gate, appearing on the in-game calendar for a few days several times per year.

### Farming Tips
To earn tokens efficiently, prepare several transmog outfits in advance that fit common Trial of Style themes like "Faction Pride" or "Epic Purple". The individual green-quality items sharing this appearance, such as the Simple Kilt and Simple Blouse, are Bind on Equip world drops from classic level 1-60 zones. These pieces can often be purchased directly from the Auction House at any time. This allows you to assemble the look without having to wait for the holiday event to purchase the official ensemble.

7.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "Bonecaster's Regalia," a Bind-on-Equip world drop set found in high-level Classic dungeons. The primary source for all pieces is humanoid and undead trash mobs (level 55-60) within instances. The highest concentration of these mobs is found in Scholomance, Stratholme, and both Upper and Lower Blackrock Spire. As these are zone-wide drops, no specific boss is tied to any specific armor slot. The main armor slots are Bonecaster's Cap (Head), Bonecaster's Spaulders (Shoulder), Bonecaster's Vest (Chest), Bonecaster's Sarong (Legs), Bonecaster's Gloves (Hands), Bonecaster's Boots (Feet), Bonecaster's Belt (Waist), and Bonecaster's Binds (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from trash mobs, so there is no daily or weekly lockout; however, you are subject to the cap of 10 instance resets per hour. The entire farm can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the low, random nature of world drops, acquiring a full set through farming could take anywhere from a few dozen to several hundred instance clears. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces are Bind-on-Equip and drop from the same pool of mobs.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to obtain this set is by searching for each piece by name on the Auction House, as other players may have farmed and listed them. If you prefer to farm it yourself, the best method is to clear large groups of trash mobs in an instance like the living side of Stratholme or the initial rooms of Scholomance. A high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage can clear the trash, exit the dungeon, right-click their character portrait to "Reset all instances," and re-enter to repeat the process quickly. No bonus rolls or other mechanics can influence the drop rate.

8.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind on Equip world drops from mobs between levels 40 and 50. The most effective way to farm these pieces is by clearing dungeons that contain a high density of mobs within this level range, such as Maraudon, Zul'Farrak, or Uldaman. As world drops, no specific boss is tied to any particular piece. The full set consists of the Aboriginal Headdress, Shoulderpads, Vest, Loincloth, Gloves, Footwraps, Sash, and Wraps. Any enemy within the appropriate level bracket, both inside and outside of dungeons, has a small chance to drop a piece.

### Farming Logistics
This farm has no traditional lockout; however, players farming dungeons are limited to 10 instance resets per hour on a single character. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drop chances, acquiring the full set through farming could take anywhere from dozens to hundreds of dungeon clears. This is not a tier set, so there are no tokens or class restrictions on the loot itself. All pieces come from the same pool of world drops, so no alternate sources need to be farmed.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip. For those who prefer to farm, use a high-mobility class with strong AoE abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear dungeons quickly. The purple and orange wings of Maraudon offer one of the highest concentrations of appropriate-level mobs per run. Check the Auction House regularly even while farming, as individual pieces may be listed for a low price, saving you significant time.

10.content:
### How to Obtain
The Willow Regalia is a world drop set obtained from killing mobs between levels 20 and 30. All pieces of the set are random drops from creatures within this level range. The most efficient farming zones include Duskwood, Ashenvale, and Hillsbrad Foothills due to their high density of humanoid and beast mobs. The set consists of seven pieces: Willow Circlet (Head), Willow Vest (Chest), Willow Pants (Legs), Willow Gloves (Hands), Willow Boots (Feet), Willow Belt (Waist), and Willow Bracers (Wrist). A matching shoulder piece does not exist for this specific set.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is farmed from respawning world mobs. It is fully soloable at any level, and trivially so by a max-level character. Because the pieces are random world drops with a low drop chance, collecting the full set via farming can take several hours of continuous mob clearing. There is no token system for this set, and all pieces come from the same general pool of world loot. The appearance is not tied to any specific raid or dungeon difficulty.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Willow Regalia are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), making the Auction House the most reliable and fastest method of acquisition. Farming is best done on a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Many other uncommon green items share the exact same appearance but have different names; the wardrobe system will unlock the Willow appearance from any of these lookalike items. Searching the Auction House for the specific appearance model rather than just the "Willow" name is a more effective strategy.

11.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind-on-Equip world drops found in level 10-20 zones from the original game. The pieces drop from any humanoid or beast mob within the appropriate level range. The most efficient zones for farming include The Barrens, Silverpine Forest, Westfall, and Darkshore due to high mob density. The specific pieces are the Aboriginal Vest (Chest), Aboriginal Loincloth (Legs), Aboriginal Gloves (Hands), Aboriginal Sandals (Feet), Aboriginal Sash (Waist), and Aboriginal Bracers (Wrist). There are no matching Head or Shoulder items in this set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with this set, as it is a world drop that can be farmed continuously. The entire set can be fully soloed at any level, though it is trivial for a max-level character. The number of kills required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can range from hundreds to thousands due to random drop chances. This is not a tier set, so there are no tokens or class restrictions on drops, though only Leather-wearing classes can learn the appearances. All pieces drop from the same sources and have no difficulty modes associated with them.

### Farming Tips
The most practical method to obtain this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House, as direct farming is unreliable. All items are Bind-on-Equip and are commonly listed for sale. If you choose to farm it directly, use a max-level character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large packs of mobs quickly. There are no skips, shortcuts, or bonus roll mechanics applicable to this type of farm.

12.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased for a small amount of copper and silver from vendors in the Human starting areas. The main vendor is Thomas Mordan, the Cloth & Leather Armor merchant located inside the Lion's Pride Inn in Goldshire, Elwynn Forest. Alternatively, Zaldimar Wefhellt in the Mage Quarter of Stormwind sells the same pieces. The set consists of the Seer's Robe (Chest), Seer's Mantle (Shoulder), Seer's Pants (Legs), Seer's Gloves (Hands), Seer's Boots (Feet), Seer's Belt (Waist), and Seer's Cuffs (Wrist). There is no matching headpiece sold by these vendors as part of the set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set as it is purchased directly from vendors. The entire appearance set can be acquired in a single trip by any character visiting the vendors. The total time to collect all pieces is under five minutes. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces are available from the same source. As it is a cosmetic cloth set, only cloth-wearing classes (Mage, Priest, Warlock) can add the appearances to their account-wide wardrobe upon purchase.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is to create a new level 1 Human character, as they spawn in Northshire Abbey, a short run from the vendor in Goldshire. A Human Warlock is ideal as they start with several pieces of the set already equipped, reducing the number of items you need to purchase. The items are Bind on Pickup and cannot be sold on the Auction House. Since the cost is negligible, simply visit the vendor on a cloth-wearing character and purchase every available "Seer's" piece to ensure the full appearance is collected.

13.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from pieces dropped in the Classic dungeons Stratholme and Upper Blackrock Spire. The Head (Mystic's Hat) and Legs (Mystic's Leggings) drop from Baron Rivendare in Stratholme's Undead wing. The Shoulders (Mystic's Pauldrons) drop from Cannon Master Willey, the Hands (Mystic's Gloves) drop from Hearthsinger Forresten, and the Feet (Mystic's Boots) drop from Postmaster Malown, all located in Stratholme's Live wing. The Chest (Mystic's Robe) drops from General Drakkisath, the final boss of Upper Blackrock Spire. The Waist (Mystic's Belt) and Wrist (Mystic's Vambraces) are zone-wide trash drops within both Upper and Lower Blackrock Spire.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons operate on a standard instance lockout, allowing for up to 10 resets per hour per account. The entire set can be easily soloed by any max-level character. Given the multiple sources and average drop chances, collecting all boss-dropped pieces may take 10-20 total runs spread across both dungeons. This set does not use a tier token system. Note that the pieces are split between two separate dungeons and three distinct wings/locations, requiring targeted farming for specific slots. These dungeons only have a single difficulty, so there are no separate lockouts to manage.

### Farming Tips
For efficiency, farm Stratholme's Live side (main gate) and Undead side (service gate) separately for their specific pieces. The waist and wrist pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), so check the Auction House first to potentially save significant farming time. When farming Baron Rivendare for the Head and Legs, you must clear the three ziggurats in the Slaughter Square to open his gate. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Monk, will significantly speed up travel time within the large instances.

14.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from humanoid mobs within the Battle for Azeroth expansion, primarily in Kul Tiras. The most effective farming location is Tiragarde Sound, specifically from the pirate and human mobs in and around the Freehold dungeon entrance. All main armor pieces drop from these sources: Buccaneer's Tricorne (Head), Buccaneer's Epaulets (Shoulder), Buccaneer's Vest (Chest), Buccaneer's Britches (Legs), Buccaneer's Gloves (Hands), Buccaneer's Boots (Feet), Buccaneer's Sash (Waist), and Buccaneer's Cuffs (Wrist). There are no specific bosses tied to these drops; any humanoid mob in the designated areas has a chance to drop a piece.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout or reset timer, allowing for continuous farming. The entire set can be fully soloed at the current maximum level, as the mobs are from a previous expansion. Due to the low drop chance of specific world BoE items, collecting the full set through farming alone can take many hours, depending on luck. There is no tier token system for this set, and classes do not share tokens. All pieces drop from the same general loot table, and instance difficulties like LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic are not applicable to this farm.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Buccaneer's Regalia are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), meaning they can be bought and sold on the Auction House, which is often the fastest way to complete the set. For farming, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect (AoE) abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs efficiently. A highly concentrated farming spot is the area directly outside the entrance to the Freehold dungeon in Tiragarde Sound. Farming in a 5-person group can increase kill speed, as each player receives their own loot from mob tags.

15.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon green-quality items that drop from level 55-60 dungeons from the Classic era. The specific pieces are found in Scholomance, Stratholme, and the Warlords of Draenor version of Upper Blackrock Spire. The Head (Sorcerer's Crown) drops from Darkmaster Gandling in Scholomance, and the Shoulders (Sorcerer's Mantle) drop from Jandice Barov in the same instance. The Chest (Sorcerer's Robes) is a drop from General Drakkisath in Upper Blackrock Spire, while the Legs (Sorcerer's Leggings) come from Baron Rivendare in Stratholme. The Hands (Sorcerer's Gloves) drop from Doctor Theolen Krastinov in Scholomance and the Feet (Sorcerer's Boots) from Magistrate Barthilas in Stratholme. The Waist (Sorcerer's Belt) and Wrist (Sorcerer's Bindings) are world drops from trash mobs within these dungeons.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons for this set have a normal instance lockout, meaning they can be reset manually up to 10 times per hour per account. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, expect to complete 5-10 runs of each required dungeon to collect the boss-specific pieces. This set does not use a tier token system. The pieces are spread across three separate instances, so you will need to farm all of them to complete the set. These dungeons do not have multiple difficulties; they only exist on a single Normal mode.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire the waist and wrist pieces is to check the Auction House, as they are Bind-on-Equip world drops and are often available for purchase. For Stratholme, remember to use both the main gate entrance for the "Living" side to reach Magistrate Barthilas and the service gate entrance for the "Undead" side to reach Baron Rivendare. To farm the chest piece, you must run the level 100 version of Upper Blackrock Spire, which was updated in Warlords of Draenor. Using a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk can significantly reduce the time spent clearing trash between bosses.

16.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a random world drop from Classic-era content. All eight pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are Bind-on-Equip and drop from humanoid, demon, and beast mobs between levels 38 and 48. There are no specific bosses or locations tied to individual pieces. The most effective zones for farming are those with high mob density in this level range, such as Tanaris, Feralas, and the Hinterlands. The entire set comes from this single source type, with no pieces obtained via quests, dungeons, or vendors.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as it drops from open-world mobs. The farm is fully soloable at current max level, as the enemies are low-level and can be defeated instantly. Because it is a random green world drop with a very large loot table, the time required to farm all pieces directly is extremely high and unpredictable, potentially taking hundreds of hours. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE). This means the most reliable source is often the Auction House, not direct farming.

### Farming Tips
The most time-efficient method to acquire this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House. If farming directly, use a class with high mobility and instant area-of-effect abilities to kill large groups of mobs in the target zones. Since all pieces are BoE, you can farm them on any character and mail them to your main or sell duplicates. Set up a shopping list on the Auction House to be notified when pieces are listed, as they can sometimes be posted for low prices by sellers unaware of their transmog value.

17.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from the Legion expansion, meaning its pieces can drop from almost any creature in the Broken Isles zones. The best farming locations are high-density areas such as Suramar City or the Broken Shore, where mobs can be defeated in large numbers. Unlike raid or dungeon sets, individual pieces like the Pagan Vestments and Pagan Shoulderpads do not drop from specific bosses. The entire eight-piece set (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) is Bind-on-Equip, making the Auction House a viable alternative acquisition method.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout; mobs can be farmed continuously. The farm is fully soloable at current maximum level, with mobs posing no threat. Due to the extremely low and random drop chance for specific world BoEs, collecting the full set via farming can take an indeterminate number of hours. There are no tier tokens involved, and all pieces come from the same global loot table within Legion content. All items are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), which means they can be traded or sold on the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by searching for "Pagan" on the Auction House, as farming for specific BoEs is highly unreliable. If you choose to farm, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage to clear mobs quickly. An identical appearance can be collected from the Looking For Raid (LFR) difficulty of the Trial of Valor raid by speaking to Archmage Timear in Dalaran (Broken Isles) to queue for it. This LFR version, "Garb of the Chosen Dead," offers a more targeted farming approach with a weekly lockout.

18.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality leather items that drop from bosses in the Classic dungeons Scholomance, Stratholme, and Upper Blackrock Spire. The Head (Pagan Circlet) drops from Darkmaster Gandling and the Shoulders (Pagan Pauldrons) drop from Jandice Barov, both in Scholomance. The Chest (Pagan Vest) is obtained from General Drakkisath in Upper Blackrock Spire. The Legs (Pagan Leggings) drop from Baron Rivendare and the Feet (Pagan Boots) drop from Magistrate Barthilas, both in Stratholme. The Hands (Pagan Gloves) are from The Unforgiven and the Wrists (Pagan Wristbands) are from the rare spawn Hearthsinger Forresten, also in Stratholme, while the Waist (Pagan Belt) is a zone drop from trash mobs within Scholomance.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons involved operate on a daily lockout, but you can also manually reset them up to 10 times per hour, making the farm highly repeatable. The entire set can be easily soloed at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per item, collecting all eight pieces could take between 10 to 20 total runs across the three dungeons, subject to luck. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct drops from bosses. Be aware that the pieces are split across Scholomance, Stratholme, and Upper Blackrock Spire, requiring you to farm all three locations.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to clear the dungeons quickly. You can manually reset each instance by exiting, right-clicking your character portrait, and selecting "Reset all instances." Remember that the Stratholme instance has two primary paths; use the main entrance for the "living" side bosses and the service entrance to go directly to the "undead" side for Baron Rivendare. All pieces in this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be bought on the Auction House.

19.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 40-50 zones and dungeons. The most concentrated and popular farming location is the Classic dungeon Scholomance, located in the Western Plaguelands. All pieces are random drops from the trash mobs within the instance. The primary set pieces are Sanguine Turban (Head), Sanguine Mantle (Shoulder), Sanguine Robe (Chest), Sanguine Trousers (Legs), Sanguine Gloves (Hands), Sanguine Sandals (Feet), Sanguine Belt (Waist), and Sanguine Cuffs (Wrist). As these are random world drops, no specific boss is guaranteed to drop any particular piece.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed in a Normal difficulty dungeon, which has no daily or weekly lockout. However, you are limited to 10 instance resets per hour on a single account. The entire dungeon can be easily soloed by a current max-level character in just a few minutes per run. Due to the completely random nature of world drops, the number of runs required is highly variable and could range from a few to hundreds. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces drop from the same source type (random mobs).

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, use a high-mobility class like a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter. The most efficient strategy is to run through Scholomance, pull as many trash mobs as possible in large groups, and defeat them with area-of-effect abilities. The most practical tip is that all pieces of the Sanguine Regalia are Bind-on-Equip (BoE). This means they can be bought and sold on the Auction House, which is often a much faster and more reliable method of completing the set than farming it directly.

20.content:

All pieces are from the same source type and there are no alternate difficulties or lockouts to consider.

### Farming Tips

Farming the items directly is highly inefficient due to the low drop chances spread across a large loot table. 21.content:
### How to Obtain
This mail set is assembled from uncommon Bind-on-Equip items that drop within level 55-60 Classic dungeons. The primary farming locations are Stratholme, Scholomance, and Upper Blackrock Spire. The Head slot, Mask of the Unforgiven, drops from Baroness Anastari in Stratholme. The Shoulders, Wyrmtongue Shoulders, drop from bosses in Upper Blackrock Spire. The Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, and Waist slots are all part of the "Cadaverous" series of items, which are random zone drops from trash and bosses within both Stratholme and Scholomance.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons for this set are subject to a daily lockout, but Normal difficulty instances can be manually reset up to 10 times per hour. The entire set can be farmed solo by a max-level character. Due to the low, random drop chance of the zone-wide pieces, collecting the full set could take anywhere from 20 to 50 instance resets. This set does not use a tier token system and there are no class restrictions on the drops. The dungeons do not have separate LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic difficulties; there is only a single difficulty level.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, which allows you to manually reset after each run. Focus on clearing large groups of trash mobs in Stratholme and Scholomance to maximize your chances at the zone-wide "Cadaverous" pieces. Since all pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip, check the Auction House frequently, as you can often purchase the entire set for gold, saving a significant amount of farming time. No special skips are required, as a high-level character can clear the instances in a few minutes.

22.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "Incanter's Regalia," a Bind-on-Equip world drop set from level 68-70+ zones and instances in Outland. The primary pieces are the Incanter's Cowl (Head), Incanter's Pauldrons (Shoulder), Incanter's Robe (Chest), Incanter's Trousers (Legs), Incanter's Gloves (Hands), and Incanter's Boots (Feet). The matching Incanter's Belt (Waist) and Incanter's Bracers (Wrist) complete the set. As these are world drops, no specific boss is tied to any piece. The most effective farming locations are areas with a high density of elite mobs, such as the trash packs within the Sunwell Plateau raid or throughout the Isle of Quel'Danas.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout; instances and zones can be farmed continuously. The farm can be fully soloed at max level with trivial difficulty. The number of runs required is highly unpredictable due to the extremely low drop chance from a large, shared loot table. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces drop from the same general sources, but they are most practically and reliably acquired via the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to obtain this set is by purchasing the individual "Incanter's" pieces from the Auction House, as targeted farming is unreliable and can take hundreds of hours. If you wish to farm the items directly, the optimal strategy is to clear the non-boss trash mobs inside the Sunwell Plateau raid, then exit and reset the instance. A class with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage, like a Monk or Druid, is ideal for clearing trash quickly. Since all pieces are Bind-on-Equip, you can farm them on any character and either mail them to the appropriate character or sell them.

23.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 35-45 mobs in Classic zones. The best zones to farm these pieces are Stranglethorn Vale, Desolace, Arathi Highlands, and Dustwallow Marsh. The set consists of six pieces: Silver-thread Armor (Chest), Silver-thread Boots (Feet), Silver-thread Gloves (Hands), Silver-thread Pants (Legs), Silver-thread Shoulders (Shoulder), and Silver-thread Sash (Waist). A high-mobility class is recommended to speed up clears if you decide to farm the world drop pieces from trash mobs. To reset the dungeon, step outside the portal, right-click your character portrait, and select "Reset all instances."

48.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from mobs between levels 40-50. The primary zones for farming these pieces are Tanaris, Feralas, and The Hinterlands, due to their high mob density in the appropriate level range. All pieces drop from the same pool of random mobs within these zones. The specific items that complete the set are the Resilient Cap (Head), Watcher's Mantle (Shoulder), Watcher's Tunic (Chest), Resilient Trousers (Legs), Resilient Handgrips (Hands), Resilient Boots (Feet), Resilient Belt (Waist), and Watcher's Cuffs (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm as it consists of killing mobs in open-world zones. The set is fully soloable by any class at current max level, as the enemies are low-level. The time required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to random drop chances, potentially taking anywhere from a few hours to several days of dedicated farming. There is no tier token system, and all pieces are individual drops from the same general loot table.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House, as all items are Bind-on-Equip (BoE). For those who prefer to farm it, use a high-mobility class with strong instant-cast Area of Effect (AoE) abilities to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on areas with high mob density and fast respawn rates, such as the Wastewander bandit camps in southern Tanaris. Check the Auction House periodically even while farming, as individual pieces may be listed for a low price.

50.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is a Bind on Equip world drop from the Shadowlands zone of Maldraxxus. All eight pieces drop randomly from humanoid and undead creatures throughout the zone, as well as from some treasure containers. The specific items are the Arachnidian Crown, Arachnidian Pauldrons, Arachnidian Tunic, Arachnidian Legguards, Arachnidian Gloves, Arachnidian Footpads, Arachnidian Cinch, and Arachnidian Wristwraps. There are no specific bosses or guaranteed sources for these items. The drop chance from any given mob is low, making targeted farming a lengthy process.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with this farm, allowing for continuous farming of mobs in the zone. The entire set can be farmed solo by a max-level character, with no group requirements. Due to the very low, random drop chance of world BoEs, acquiring the full set through farming could require killing hundreds or thousands of mobs. No tier tokens are involved in this set's acquisition. All pieces originate from the same source and have a similar drop chance.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by purchasing the Bind on Equip pieces from the Auction House. For self-farming, focus on areas with high mob density and fast respawn rates, such as the House of Constructs or the floating necropolis of Zolramus. Use a class with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage to maximize kills per hour. Consider farming in a group to increase the overall number of mobs killed, though loot is personal.

51.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the reward from the "Garments of the Archmage" quest line, which upgrades the Tier 0 Magister's Regalia set. The chain begins with Deliana in Ironforge for Alliance or Mok'var in Orgrimmar for Horde. To obtain each piece, you must first acquire the corresponding piece of the Magister's Regalia, which drops from bosses in Scholomance, Stratholme, and Upper Blackrock Spire. You then turn in the Tier 0 item along with specific reagents to receive a quest, which sends you back into high-level classic dungeons like Dire Maul or Blackrock Spire to complete an objective. Upon completion, you are rewarded with the upgraded Abjurer's Regalia piece.

### Farming Logistics
The primary farm is for the prerequisite Magister's Regalia set, which drops from dungeons on a daily lockout. All required dungeons, including Scholomance, Stratholme, and Blackrock Spire, are fully soloable at max level. Acquiring the full 8-piece Magister's set may take 10-20 total dungeon runs, after which the quest chain itself can be completed in a few hours. This set does not use a token system; the initial pieces are direct boss drops exclusive to the Mage class and the final pieces are quest rewards. These Classic dungeons only have one difficulty, and there are no separate lockouts to consider.

### Farming Tips
Farm the entire 8-piece Magister's Regalia set before starting the quest chain. Purchase all required reagents from the Auction House in advance to avoid interrupting your progress; these include items like Runecloth, Dark Runes, and Essence of Undeath. Once you have the full Tier 0 set and all reagents, pick up all eight upgrade quests simultaneously and complete the objectives by zone to minimize travel time. While the armor pieces themselves are Bind on Pickup, the trade goods needed for the quests are not, making the Auction House your fastest source for them.

52.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of random world drop items and is not obtained from a specific raid or vendor. All pieces share the "Hibernal" prefix and can drop from mobs and bosses inside level 40-50 Classic dungeons. The primary dungeons for farming this set are Maraudon, Uldaman, and Zul'Farrak. Due to high mob density, Maraudon is the most efficient instance to farm for the complete set. The specific pieces are the Hibernal Cowl (Head), Hibernal Mantle (Shoulder), Hibernal Robe (Chest), Hibernal Pants (Legs), Hibernal Gloves (Hands), Hibernal Boots (Feet), Hibernal Sash (Waist), and Hibernal Bracers (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
This farm is subject to the standard 10 instances per hour, per account, limit for Normal dungeons. The set can be fully soloed at current max level by any class. Due to the items being random drops from a large loot table, collecting all eight pieces can take 20-50 instance clears or more, making the farm highly dependent on random chance. This is not a tier set, so there are no tokens or class restrictions on the drops. All pieces drop from the same pool of mobs within the recommended dungeons.

### Farming Tips
As all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), the most efficient way to acquire this set is to purchase the items from the Auction House; search for "Hibernal" to see all available pieces. For players who prefer to farm it, choose a class with high movement speed and area-of-effect abilities, such as a Monk or Druid, to clear trash packs quickly. In Maraudon, run a loop through the Orange and Purple wings, then exit and reset the instance. There are no skips or alternative difficulties, and systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply to this content.

53.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-set items that drop from various level 50-60 dungeons in Classic. The headpiece, Crimson Felt Hat, drops from Baron Rivendare in Stratholme. The shoulders, Boreal Mantle, drop from Ras Frostwhisper in Scholomance. Robes of the Royal Crown drop from Emperor Dagran Thaurissan in Blackrock Depths, and the Ban'thok Sash drops from the arena event chest spawned by Ok'thor the Breaker in the same dungeon. The hands, legs, and feet (Hands of Power, Skyshroud Leggings, Omnicast Boots) drop from Quartermaster Zigris, Highlord Omokk, and Goraluk Anvilcrack respectively, all within Lower Blackrock Spire. The wrist slot, Cindercloth Bracers, is a Bind-on-Equip item crafted by Tailors.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing these items are subject to a daily lockout for Heroic difficulty, but since these are Classic dungeons, they only have a Normal mode which can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The instances can be fully soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Given an average drop chance of 15-25% per boss, collecting all dungeon-dropped pieces will likely require 5-10 targeted kills for each specific boss. This set does not use a tier token system; each piece drops directly as an item. The pieces are spread across four different dungeons, with the wrist being a crafted item, requiring multiple farming locations.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest farming, enter the Normal difficulty version of each dungeon, proceed directly to the required boss, and reset the instance after the kill; you can do this up to 10 times per hour. The wrist piece, Cindercloth Bracers, is Bind-on-Equip and can often be purchased from the Auction House, which is faster than gathering the materials to craft it. In Blackrock Depths, using the Shadowforge Key allows you to open shortcuts directly to the Emperor's throne room. For Stratholme, use the service entrance to access the Undead side containing Baron Rivendare more quickly.

55.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Uncommon quality Bind on Equip items that are world drops from level 40-55 mobs in Classic zones. The specific items are the Nightscape Headband (Head), Nightscape Shoulders (Shoulder), Nightscape Tunic (Chest), Nightscape Pants (Legs), Nightscape Gloves (Hands), Nightscape Boots (Feet), Nightscape Belt (Waist), and Nightscape Wristbands (Wrist). Optimal farming zones include Tanaris, Feralas, The Hinterlands, and the Burning Steppes. Mobs within dungeons of a similar level range, such as Zul'Farrak and Blackrock Depths, can also drop these pieces.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is farmed from world mobs. The entire farm is fully soloable at maximum level. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drops, collecting all pieces through farming alone can take thousands of mob kills and an unpredictable amount of time. There is no tier token system for this set. As all pieces are Bind on Equip, the most reliable and often fastest way to acquire the set is by purchasing the items directly from the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
Use a class with high mobility and potent area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs efficiently. The most practical acquisition strategy is to check the Auction House regularly for each piece, as prices can fluctuate significantly. Farming raw gold and buying the pieces is a more time-efficient method than relying on random drops. Any other valuable BoE items you find while farming can be sold to fund your purchase of the set.

56.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon leather items that drop from the Scholomance dungeon. The main pieces drop from specific bosses, while the remaining slots are random drops from trash mobs throughout the instance. The Headmaster's Headdress drops from the final boss, Darkmaster Gandling, and the Cadaverous Armor drops from Instructor Chillheart. Jandice Barov drops the Cadaverous Leggings, Lilian Voss drops the Cadaverous Walkers, and Rattlegore drops the Cadaverous Gloves. The Cadaverous Epaulets, Cadaverous Belt, and Bone-Clasped Bracers drop from any non-boss enemy within the dungeon.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed on a personal loot basis with a daily dungeon lockout on Heroic, but Normal difficulty can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The entire instance is easily soloable by any max-level character, with clears taking only a few minutes. As there is no tier token system, all pieces are direct drops for any leather-wearing class. Due to the number of pieces and reliance on both boss and random trash drops, collecting the full set can take anywhere from 10 to 20 runs. The set only drops from the Normal difficulty version of Scholomance. Note that the shoulder, waist, and wrist pieces are zone-wide drops from non-boss enemies, not from specific bosses.

### Farming Tips
Run the dungeon exclusively on Normal difficulty, as the recolored set pieces do not appear in the Heroic loot table. To farm efficiently, perform a full clear of all trash and bosses, then exit the instance, right-click your character portrait, and select "Reset all instances" to begin again. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Using a class with high mobility and area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, will significantly speed up the time it takes to complete each run.

57.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Uncommon quality world drops from level 30-40 humanoid mobs in Classic zones. All pieces are Bind on Equip. The most concentrated farming locations are the Syndicate encampments in northeastern Arathi Highlands, specifically around Stromgarde Keep, and the Witherbark Troll villages in the southern part of the same zone. Mobs such as Syndicate Shadow Mages and Witherbark Shadow Hunters are common sources. The primary pieces to look for are the Shadow Council Robe, Mantle, and Cowl, though the entire 8-piece set drops from this level range.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it involves killing respawning world mobs. The farm can be fully soloed by any max-level character with ease. Due to the extremely low drop rates for specific world drops from a large loot table, collecting all pieces manually could take thousands of mob kills and many hours of dedicated farming. This is not a tier set, so there are no tokens and no class restrictions on loot. All pieces drop from the same general pool of level 30-40 humanoids.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by searching for individual pieces on the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip. For direct farming, use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk with strong area-of-effect abilities to clear camps quickly. Create a circular route through the Syndicate camps in Arathi Highlands to continuously farm as mobs respawn. Because this is a Classic zone world drop, modern systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply.

58.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is an Uncommon quality Bind-on-Equip world drop from level 55-60 undead mobs in Classic zones. The most effective farming locations are the Eastern Plaguelands and Western Plaguelands. The dungeon Scholomance also has a high density of appropriate mobs that can drop the pieces. All eight pieces—Bonecaster's Cap, Spaulders, Vest, Leggings, Gloves, Boots, Belt, and Binds—drop randomly from any of these sources rather than from specific bosses.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming mobs in the open world zones. The Scholomance dungeon instance has a daily reset for characters over level 60. The entire farm can be fully soloed at the current maximum level. No tier tokens are used, and all pieces come from the same general loot table.

### Farming Tips
Use a class with high mobility and AoE damage, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, to clear large packs of mobs in the Plaguelands quickly. Since every piece is Bind-on-Equip (BoE), the fastest method is to check the Auction House, where individual pieces or the full set can often be purchased. Farming inside Scholomance can be more efficient than the open world due to the mob density and lack of competition from other players. The appearance is only usable by cloth-wearing classes (Mage, Priest, Warlock), but any class can farm the BoE items to sell or send to an alt.

59.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind on Equip world drops from mobs in The Burning Crusade content, primarily levels 60-70. All pieces for the main slots—Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist—are obtained as random drops. The items share appearances but have different names, such as "Sage's," "Sorcerer's," or "Seer's." High-density dungeons like Hellfire Ramparts, The Blood Furnace, and Mana-Tombs are effective farming spots. Open-world farming in zones like Hellfire Peninsula or Terokkar Forest by targeting humanoid and demon mobs is also a viable source.

### Farming Logistics
Farming this set has no lockout in the open world, but dungeon farming is subject to the 10-instances-per-hour account-wide cap. The farm is fully soloable at max level with trivial difficulty. The number of runs or hours needed is entirely dependent on random drop chance and cannot be reliably estimated. This set does not use a tier token system, and there are no raid lockouts or difficulty settings (like LFR/Normal/Heroic) that affect its acquisition. All pieces drop from the same general pool of Outland mobs.

### Farming Tips
For dungeon farming, always run on Normal difficulty to clear as fast as possible, as higher difficulties do not increase the drop rate of these specific items. Since every piece is Bind on Equip (BoE), the most efficient way to acquire the set is often by searching the Auction House. Use search terms like "Sage's," "Sorcerer's," and filter by armor type and level range (60-70) to find the pieces. A class with high mobility and instant area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, will significantly speed up clearing large groups of enemies.

60.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is sourced from various dungeons in The Burning Crusade expansion on Heroic difficulty. The head piece, Mask of the Deceiver, drops from Vazruden the Herald in Heroic Hellfire Ramparts. The Shoulderpads of the Stranger drop from Murmur in Heroic Shadow Labyrinth, and the Tunic of the Nightwatcher drops from Warchief Kargath Bladefist in Heroic The Shattered Halls. Both the Gloves of the Unbound and Leggings of the Sly drop in Heroic The Underbog, from The Black Stalker and Hungarfen respectively. The final piece, Boots of the Unjust, drops from Broggok in Heroic Blood Furnace. This set does not include a visually matching waist or wrist piece from the same sources.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing these items are on a daily Heroic dungeon lockout, meaning each can be cleared once per day per character. All required dungeons are fully soloable by any class at current max level. Assuming an average drop chance of 20% per item, it will take approximately 5-10 daily clears of each specific dungeon to collect the full set. This is a direct-drop set and does not use a tier token system. The pieces are spread across five different dungeons, requiring travel between Hellfire Peninsula, Zangarmarsh, and Auchindoun.

### Farming Tips
Run these dungeons on Heroic difficulty, as this is the only mode where this specific recolor drops. Use a character with high mobility to minimize clear times; setting your Hearthstone to Shattrath City provides central access to all Outland zones. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be acquired from the Auction House. The key requirements to enter Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons were removed, allowing unrestricted access for any character level 70 or higher.

61.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the reward from the Classic-era Dungeon Set 2 upgrade questline. To begin, you must first collect the full 8-piece "Magister's Regalia" (Dungeon Set 1), which drops from various bosses in Stratholme, Scholomance, and Blackrock Spire. With the prerequisite set pieces, you start a quest chain from an NPC in either Ironforge or Orgrimmar. Each piece of the Arcane Regalia is a quest reward for turning in the corresponding Magister's piece, along with expensive trade goods like Arcane Crystals, Dark Iron Ore, and Flasks of Supreme Power. The quests also require you to venture back into these dungeons to kill specific bosses for quest items.

### Farming Logistics
The prerequisite set pieces are farmed from dungeons on a daily reset lockout. All required dungeons and quest objectives are easily soloable by a max-level character. Acquiring the full Tier 0 set can take 10-20 dungeon runs due to drop rate variance; the subsequent quest chain can be completed in a few hours once all materials are gathered. This set does not use a token system, and the classes that share this specific leather appearance are Rogue, Druid, Monk, and Demon Hunter. The prerequisite "Magister's Regalia" set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be acquired from any other source.

### Farming Tips
Run the classic dungeons on Normal difficulty, as this is the only difficulty available. It is most efficient to farm the complete 8-piece "Magister's Regalia" set before starting the upgrade questline. To save time, purchase all required crafting reagents from the Auction House before beginning the quests. Because the quest chain is long and requires significant travel, setting your Hearthstone to a major city like Orgrimmar or Stormwind is recommended.

62.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality Bind on Equip (BoE) world drop items found throughout Outland. The pieces do not drop from specific bosses but are random drops from trash mobs inside Burning Crusade dungeons and from creatures in outdoor zones. Effective farming locations include instances with high trash density like Mana-Tombs, Auchenai Crypts, and Sethekk Halls. The main cosmetic pieces are Nether-Stalker's Headpiece (Head), Pauldrons of the Thousand-Moons (Shoulder), Fel-Chain Hauberk (Chest), Greaves of the Iron-Guardian (Legs), Gauntlets of the Bold (Hands), and Boots of the Outlander (Feet).

The farm has no lockout when killing mobs in open-world zones; when farming dungeons, Normal difficulty instances can be reset up to 10 times per hour. Due to the extremely low and variable drop chance of specific world drops, collecting the full set via farming can take hundreds of dungeon runs or thousands of individual mob kills. This is not a tier set, so it does not use a token system and has no class restrictions on drops. All pieces come from the same source type but can also be purchased from the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to check the Auction House frequently, as all pieces are Bind on Equip and can be purchased directly from other players. If farming, run Normal difficulty dungeons like Mana-Tombs, as they can be reset multiple times per hour. A common strategy is to clear the initial trash packs in a loop, exit the instance, reset it, and repeat. Use a character with high mobility and large area-of-effect abilities to clear mobs as quickly as possible.

64.content:

65.content:

67.content:

68.content:

69.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from Quartermaster Koro'lan in Lion's Watch, Tanaan Jungle, after reaching Exalted reputation with the Warlords of Draenor faction Laughing Skull Orcs. This faction is exclusive to Horde players. The entire set is sold as a single item, "Ensemble: Laughing Skull Regalia," for 5000 gold. The ensemble teaches the appearances for the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Waist, Legs, Hands, and Feet slots. There is no matching wrist piece in this set.

### Farming Logistics
The set is obtained via a reputation grind which has no daily or weekly lockout. This farm can be fully soloed at max level by repeatedly killing specific mobs in Draenor. Reaching Exalted requires earning 42,000 reputation points through combat. While any Horde character can earn the reputation and purchase the ensemble, the appearances are Plate and can only be used for transmog by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. The set is not obtained via tier tokens and all pieces are from the single vendor source.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient reputation farm is killing the level 100 mobs in the Everbloom Wilds area of northern Gorgrond. Building a Level 2 or 3 Trading Post in your Garrison provides a 20% bonus to all Draenor reputation gains, significantly speeding up the process. Reputation-boosting items, such as a Darkmoon Top Hat, can also be used to accelerate the grind. The ensemble is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

71.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of uncommon world drops found primarily within the Temple of Atal'Hakkar dungeon, also known as Sunken Temple. While the pieces are not part of an official, named set, they share a distinct visual theme. The Head and Shoulder pieces drop from the final bosses, Jammal'an the Prophet and the Shade of Eranikus. The Chest and Legs are most commonly found on the six troll mini-bosses in the lower basin, such as Zolo and Loro. Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist items are random drops from any of the elite Atal'ai trolls populating the instance.

### Farming Logistics
The Temple of Atal'Hakkar is a dungeon and does not have a weekly lockout; it can be reset up to five times per hour per account. The instance is fully soloable by any class at current maximum level, with mobs posing no threat. Given an approximate 15-25% drop chance for each piece from its associated loot table, collecting the full set will take an average of 15-20 full clears. Classic dungeons do not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same location. The instance only has one difficulty, so multiple lockout types do not apply.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, enter the dungeon and proceed directly down into the circular lower basin, clearing the six troll bosses there first. Run a circular path around the basin to pull all elite troll packs to acquire the random drops, then proceed to the final boss area for Jammal'an and Eranikus. All pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so check the Auction House regularly to purchase any missing items directly. There are no skips in this dungeon, and mechanics can be ignored entirely by a max-level character.

72.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Alliance appearance of the cloth armor from the Battle of Dazar'alor raid in Battle for Azeroth. The eight main pieces of the set drop from specific bosses within the instance. The Head drops from High Tinker Mekkatorque, Shoulders drop from the Conclave of the Chosen, and the Chest drops from the Jadefire Masters. The Legs are found on Opulence, while both the Hands and Waist are on Grong, the Revenant's loot table. Finally, both the Feet and Wrists drop from the Champion of the Light.

### Farming Logistics
The Battle of Dazar'alor raid has a weekly lockout per difficulty, per character. All bosses are easily soloable at the current maximum level. Collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 5-10 weekly resets on a single character, given a standard 15-25% drop chance per item. This set does not use a tier token system; loot drops directly for cloth-wearing classes: Mage, Priest, and Warlock. The Looking for Raid, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts that can be completed once per week, each offering a different color variation of the set.

### Farming Tips
For the most efficient farm, run the raid on Normal difficulty, as it provides the fastest clears without the added complexity of Heroic or Mythic mechanics. You can significantly speed up acquisition by running the raid on multiple cloth-wearing characters each week. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The bonus roll system from this expansion, Seals of Wartorn Fate, is no longer functional and cannot be used to gain extra loot chances.

74.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from uncommon quality items found within the Classic 20-player raid, Zul'Gurub. The Grinning Skull Mask (Head) drops from Bloodlord Mandokir and the Grave-Dusted Pauldrons (Shoulder) are looted from High Priestess Jeklik. High Priest Thekal drops the Ribcage of Maniacal Laughter (Chest), while High Priestess Arlokk drops the Legguards of the Cackling Stalker (Legs). The Hex-Stained Handwraps (Hands) are a reward from Jin'do the Hexxer, and the Girdle of Unrestrained Glee (Waist) drops from any of the bosses summoned at the Edge of Madness event. The Muck-Caked Stompers (Feet) can be fished from the pools where Gahz'ranka is summoned, and the Bindings of the Mad God (Wrist) are a zone-wide drop from any non-boss enemy inside the instance.

### Farming Logistics
The raid instance of Zul'Gurub operates on a 3-day raid lockout, allowing for more frequent farming attempts than standard weekly raids. The entire instance is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, a player can expect to complete the 7 boss-drop pieces in approximately 10-15 lockouts. This set does not use a tier token system, so all pieces are direct drops. The wrist piece is a random drop from trash mobs rather than a specific boss. As a Classic-era raid, Zul'Gurub has only one difficulty and one loot table.

### Farming Tips
To speed up the farm, you can skip any bosses that have already dropped their respective set piece for you. The Bindings of the Mad God for the wrist slot are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be traded or purchased directly from the Auction House, which is often the fastest way to acquire them. Using a class with high mobility, such as a Druid or Monk, is recommended to navigate the large distances between bosses efficiently. While farming for this set, you also have a chance to loot the Swift Razzashi Raptor and Swift Zulian Tiger mounts from Bloodlord Mandokir and High Priest Thekal.

75.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is purchased from Temel, the Ember Court Quartermaster in Revendreth. The currency required is Infused Ruby, which is earned by successfully hosting the weekly Ember Court scenario available to members of the Venthyr Covenant. Each piece has a specific cost: Head and Shoulders cost 150 rubies each, Chest and Legs cost 200 rubies each, and the Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist cost 100 rubies each. Unlocking the ability to purchase each piece is gated behind reaching "Best Friend" reputation status with specific guests of the Ember Court.

### Farming Logistics
The primary source of Infused Rubies, the Ember Court, is on a weekly lockout per character. This activity is a solo scenario and does not require a group. Acquiring the full set is a long-term goal, as it is gated by weekly reputation gains with multiple guests, which will take several months to complete. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces are obtained from the same vendor using the same currency, and the scenario has only one difficulty level.

### Farming Tips
The most effective way to accelerate this farm is to run the Ember Court on multiple Venthyr-aligned characters each week, as the currency is character-specific but the transmog appearances are account-wide once learned. Following an online guide for the Ember Court scenario will help maximize guest happiness, leading to faster reputation gains and higher Infused Ruby rewards per run. The items are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Funneling anima to the Gilded Chalice location via the Anima Conductor can sometimes unlock a daily quest that rewards additional Infused Rubies.

76.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind on Equip items that drop from creatures throughout Pandaria. The best locations for farming are areas with high mob density, such as the frog ponds on the western side of the Timeless Isle, the Guo-Lai Halls in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms, or the dinosaur bone farming areas on the Isle of Giants. All pieces drop from the same general loot table of level 85-90 creatures in Mists of Pandaria zones. The specific items are Astralaan Headdress (Head), Astralaan Shoulderpads (Shoulder), Astralaan Tunic (Chest), Astralaan Leggings (Legs), Astralaan Gloves (Hands), Astralaan Belt (Waist), Astralaan Bracers (Wrist), and Astralaan Boots (Feet).

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout; mobs can be farmed continuously. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level with ease. Because drop rates are low and rely on random chance, collecting the full set through farming could take anywhere from a few hours to over a hundred, depending on luck. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces come from the same world drop loot table. There are no difficulty settings like LFR, Normal, or Heroic for this type of content.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by searching for individual pieces on the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip and are frequently listed by other players. For those who prefer to farm, using a character with high mobility and strong AoE capabilities, such as a Druid or Monk, will speed up clears. Farming in a 5-person group can force faster mob respawns, increasing your total number of kills per hour. Potions of luck, while discontinued, may still be in player inventories and can be used to generate additional loot containers from Pandaria mobs.

77.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted using the Protoform Synthesis system in Zereth Mortis. Each of the eight main armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) is created using the same recipe type at the Protoform Synthesis forge. Crafting a piece requires one Regal Protoform Lattice, one Glimmer of Focus, and a quantity of Genesis Motes. Regal Protoform Lattices drop from rare creatures and treasures in Zereth Mortis. Glimmers of Focus are primarily looted from Devourer-type enemies throughout the zone. Genesis Motes are a common material dropped by nearly all enemies in Zereth Mortis.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as all components are obtained from repeatable activities and creatures in an open-world zone. The entire set can be farmed solo at the current maximum level. Collecting all the necessary Lattices and Glimmers can take several hours of dedicated farming, depending on drop rate luck. This is not a token-based set, and all pieces are created through the same crafting method. Protoform Synthesis must first be unlocked by completing the "Protoform Synthesis" quest, which becomes available during Chapter 3 of the "Secrets of the First Ones" campaign.

### Farming Tips
To begin, unlock flying in Zereth Mortis via the "Unlocking the Secrets" achievement to travel quickly between rare spawns. For Regal Protoform Lattices, use the in-game group finder to join "rare farm" groups that cycle through all active rare spawns efficiently. The best source for the Glimmer of Focus is farming Devourer enemies at the Spawning Grounds in the northwest or around the Path of Inception. None of the components or the final crafted items are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

78.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from level 52-60 mobs in the original Classic zones. The most effective farming locations include Felwood, Winterspring, Western Plaguelands, and Eastern Plaguelands. Mobs in the demon-heavy area of Jaedenar in northern Felwood are a thematically appropriate and efficient source. All major pieces, including the Shadow Council's Hood, Pauldrons, Tunic, Pants, Gloves, Boots, and Sash, drop randomly from this loot pool and are not tied to specific bosses.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop farm, there is no instance lockout or daily reset; it can be farmed continuously. The set is fully soloable at the current max level, with all mobs being trivially easy to defeat. The number of kills required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to random drop chances and can range from hundreds to many thousands. All pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be freely traded and sold. This set does not use a tier token system and is not affected by raid or dungeon difficulties.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House, as direct farming is unreliable and time-consuming. For those who choose to farm, use a high-mobility class with instant area-of-effect abilities like a Druid or Monk to clear large groups of mobs quickly. You can create level 1 characters on other servers to check their Auction Houses for lower prices or better availability. Focusing on areas with high mob density and fast respawn rates will maximize your chances.

84.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of lookalike pieces from multiple level 55-60 Classic dungeons. The primary instances required are Scholomance, Blackrock Depths, and Blackrock Spire. The Head (Spellweaver's Turban) drops from General Drakkisath in Upper Blackrock Spire. The Chest (Robes of the Royal Crown) and Feet (Omnicast Boots) drop from Emperor Dagran Thaurissan and Golem Lord Argelmach respectively in Blackrock Depths. The Legs (Skyshroud Leggings) and Hands (Hands of Power) are found in Lower Blackrock Spire from Highlord Omokk and Quartermaster Zigris. The Shoulder (Kentic Amice) and Waist (Dust-Mite Cinch) drop from Darkmaster Gandling and Ras Frostwhisper in Scholomance, while the Wrist (Arcanist's Bindings) drops from The Unforgiven in Stratholme.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing these items operate on a daily dungeon reset lockout. All four dungeons (Scholomance, Blackrock Depths, Blackrock Spire, Stratholme) can be fully soloed with ease by a maximum-level character. Because the pieces are spread across multiple instances, a complete collection will likely take several daily lockouts. This set does not use a tier token system; items drop directly as loot and can be equipped by any of the specified cloth-wearing classes. There are no alternate LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic difficulties for these dungeons; only one version exists.

### Farming Tips
Use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, to quickly traverse the instances. For Blackrock Depths, use the mole machine inside the entrance to teleport directly to the "Grim Guzzler" area, which provides a significant shortcut to the bosses who drop the Chest and Feet pieces. Be aware that the Wrist piece drops from The Unforgiven, a rare spawn in the Stratholme main gate entrance, which may require multiple resets to find. All items in this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

88.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of lookalike pieces that drop from various bosses in Level 80 Heroic dungeons from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and drop directly from the specified bosses. There is no associated reputation or currency vendor for this set.

*   **Head:** Cowl of the Dire Wolf (Loken, Halls of Lightning) or Cowl of the Lithe (Grand Magus Telestra, The Nexus)
*   **Shoulder:** Mantle of the Woda (Skadi the Ruthless, Utgarde Pinnacle) or Mantle of Gnarled Stitching (Ormorok the Tree-Shaper, The Nexus)
*   **Chest:** Robes of Hoarse Cries (Anub'arak, Azjol-Nerub) or Robes of Crackling Flames (Keristrasza, The Nexus)
*   **Legs:** Breeches of the Caller (Jedoga Shadowseeker, Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom) or Leggings of the Ruins Dweller (Cyanigosa, The Violet Hold)
*   **Hands:** Gloves of Distorted Time (Chrono-Lord Epoch, The Culling of Stratholme) or Gloves of Glistening Runes (Ley-Guardian Eregos, The Oculus)
*   **Feet:** Footwraps of Teleportation (Lavanthor, The Violet Hold) or Boots of the Worshipper (Herald Volazj, Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom)
*   **Waist:** Sash of the Servant (Svala Sorrowgrave, Utgarde Pinnacle) or Belt of Unified Souls (Meathook, The Culling of Stratholme)
*   **Wrist:** Cuffs of the Shadow Ascendant (Volkhan, Halls of Lightning) or Azure-Lined Cuffs (Moragg, The Violet Hold)

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from Heroic dungeons, which are subject to a daily dungeon reset. Each character can loot each specific boss once per day. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at current max level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all 8 pieces on a single character will likely take 1-2 weeks of clearing the relevant dungeons daily. There is no tier token system for this set, as the items drop directly. All pieces come from the same type of source (WotLK Heroic dungeons), though they are spread across many different instances.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering, as these pieces do not drop on Normal. Farming on multiple cloth-wearing characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock) is the most effective strategy, as each character has a separate daily lockout. To maximize efficiency, prioritize dungeons that drop multiple desired pieces, such as The Nexus, The Violet Hold, and Halls of Lightning. Use a high-mobility character to speed up clears and skip unnecessary trash packs between bosses. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

89.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from various bosses within level 85 Cataclysm Heroic dungeons. The Head, Helmet of the Inward Eye, drops from High Priestess Azil in The Stonecore. The Shoulders, Spaulders of the Endless Plains, drop from Asaad in The Vortex Pinnacle. The Chestguard of the Lasher drops from Lady Naz'jar in Throne of the Tides, and the Gloves of the Passing Night drop from Admiral Ripsnarl in The Deadmines. The Leggings of the Impenitent drop from Lord Godfrey in Shadowfang Keep, the Boots of the Predator drop from Forgemaster Throngus in Grim Batol, and the Belt of the Dim Forest drops from General Husam in The Lost City of the Tol'vir.

### Farming Logistics
The set pieces drop from dungeons that are on a daily heroic lockout, meaning each dungeon can only be looted for these items once per day per character. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level with no difficulty. Given the seven different items dropping from seven different dungeons, collecting the full set will take a minimum of one day but will likely require multiple daily resets due to drop-rate variance. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces are personal loot from the specified bosses. The required pieces only drop on Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Set all dungeons to Heroic difficulty before entering to ensure the correct loot tables are active. Use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear the instances as quickly as possible. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. There are no relevant skips needed as a max-level character can run straight to each boss, and no special items like bonus rolls apply to this content.

93.content:
### How to Obtain
This set was acquired from Quartermaster Rez'thol in Icecrown during the Shadowlands pre-patch event, "Death's Rising." The currency used was Argent Commendations, which were rewarded from the event-specific daily quests. The full set, covering Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots, was purchased directly from this vendor. As this was a limited-time event, both the vendor and the currency were removed from the game when the event concluded, making the set permanently unobtainable.

### Farming Logistics
Acquisition was originally tied to a daily quest reset during the pre-patch event, not a raid or dungeon lockout. The quests were designed to be completed solo by players at the appropriate level. The set did not use a token system; each piece was purchased individually for Argent Commendations. All pieces came exclusively from the event vendor. This farming method is no longer available in the game.

### Farming Tips
This set cannot be farmed, as the "Death's Rising" event is no longer active. The items were Bind-on-Pickup, so they are not available on the standard Auction House. This set is also not known to appear on the Black Market Auction House. Players who did not acquire this appearance during the event must seek alternative transmog sets.

94.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from uncommon quality items dropped in three separate Cataclysm dungeons. The majority of the set drops in The Stonecore: Helm of the Inward Eye (Corborus), Pauldrons of the High Chamberlain (High Priestess Azil), Breastplate of the Stilled Heart (Ozruk), Bracers of Crushing Resolution (Slabhide), and Legguards of the Unseeing (Corborus). Blackrock Caverns contains the Gloves of Brawn (Rom'og Bonecrusher) and Boots of Ferromancy (Karsh Steelbender). The final piece, Belt of the Still Stream, drops from Lady Naz'jar in Throne of the Tides.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons can be farmed on Normal difficulty, which has no lockout and can be reset up to 10 times per hour. Alternatively, they can be run on Heroic difficulty, which has a daily lockout per character. The entire set is easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces could take between 5 to 15 total runs across the three dungeons. There is no tier token system for this set as the items drop directly from bosses.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty to bypass the daily lockout. A high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk can significantly reduce clear times. Since five of the eight pieces drop from The Stonecore, focus your farming efforts there first before moving to Blackrock Caverns and Throne of the Tides. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be purchased from the Auction House. To reset Normal instances, step outside, right-click your character portrait, and select "Reset all instances."

99.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from the Shadowlands expansion, primarily obtained by killing random mobs in the four main leveling zones: Bastion, Maldraxxus, Ardenweald, and Revendreth. All pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be traded or sold on the Auction House. The set consists of the Deathsilk Cowl (Head), Deathsilk Mantle (Shoulder), Deathsilk Robes (Chest), Deathsilk Bracelets (Wrist), Deathsilk Gloves (Hands), Deathsilk Sash (Waist), Deathsilk Leggings (Legs), and Deathsilk Slippers (Feet). There is no specific boss or location for any individual piece; they are all part of the general zone-drop loot table.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it relies on killing respawning mobs in outdoor zones. The entire farm is fully soloable at the current maximum level. Due to the random nature of world drops and the large loot tables in Shadowlands zones, acquiring a full set through farming alone can take many hours. All pieces drop from the same sources and are not tied to any tier token system. Since the items are Bind on Equip, the most reliable acquisition method is often the Auction House, bypassing the need for farming entirely.

### Farming Tips
Check the Auction House first, as purchasing the full set is almost always faster than farming for it. If you choose to farm, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect (AoE) abilities to clear large groups of enemies quickly. Look for areas with high mob density, sometimes referred to as "hyperspawn" locations, to maximize kills per hour. Farming in a group can increase efficiency, allowing for a constant rotation of mob clearing.

100.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained by a cloth-wearing character aligned with the Necrolord Covenant in Shadowlands. The main armor pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Waist) are rewarded for completing chapters of the Necrolord Covenant Campaign, starting with the quest "A Purpose in Death". The remaining pieces (Hands, Feet, Wrist) are unlocked by channeling anima to specific locations using a fully upgraded Anima Conductor within the Seat of the Primus. The Deathsilk Gloves appearance is unlocked from the Construct's Custom Coffer in the House of Constructs. The Deathsilk Slippers appearance is unlocked by defeating the rare mob Gieger in the House of Plagues, and the Deathsilk Wristwraps appearance is unlocked by defeating the rare mob Sabriel the Bonecleaver in the House of Constructs. Once an appearance is unlocked, the cosmetic item can be purchased from Goreguts in the Seat of the Primus for Reservoir Anima, though this is not required to use the transmog.

### Farming Logistics
The campaign quest portion of this set has no lockout and can be completed once per character. The Anima Conductor portion operates on a daily reset, as the available rare mobs and treasures at a channeled location change each day. The entire farm is fully soloable at current max level. Collecting the campaign pieces takes a few hours, while the Anima Conductor pieces may take several days to a week, depending on the daily rotation RNG. This set does not use a tier token system. The main set pieces come from one-time quests, while the offset pieces come from a repeatable daily activity, making for two distinct sources.

### Farming Tips
To begin, join the Necrolord Covenant on any cloth character in Oribos and complete the nine chapters of the covenant campaign to acquire the main set pieces. To acquire the remaining pieces, you must upgrade your Anima Conductor sanctum feature, which requires a significant amount of Reservoir Anima. Prioritize channeling anima to the House of Constructs and House of Plagues each day and check for the required rare or treasure.
