Once an appearance is unlocked through its associated source, it is permanently added to your account-wide collection, and you do not need to spend anima to buy the physical item from the vendor.

103.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is obtained by purchasing the "Ensemble: Deathsilk Regalia" from Temel, the quartermaster for The Ember Court in Revendreth. The Ember Court is the unique feature of the Venthyr Covenant from the Shadowlands expansion. The ensemble costs 3,000 Infused Ruby, the currency earned by hosting Ember Court scenarios. To unlock the ability to purchase the ensemble, you must first achieve the "Best Friend" reputation level with Polemarch Adrestes, one of the court's guests. The ensemble teaches the appearances for all armor slots simultaneously.

### Farming Logistics
The Ember Court scenario, the source of the required currency and reputation, is on a weekly lockout per character. This content is entirely soloable at current max level, as it was designed as single-player content during Shadowlands. Acquiring the necessary 3,000 Infused Ruby and reaching "Best Friend" status with Polemarch Adrestes will take a minimum of 5-10 weekly lockouts, depending on scenario performance. There is no tier token system; the ensemble is purchased directly with currency. All pieces are learned from the single ensemble item, with no parts coming from other sources.

### Farming Tips
To accelerate the farm, focus on inviting Polemarch Adrestes every week and selecting scenario events that cater to his preferences for combat and honor to maximize reputation gain. Using the "Wargames" RSVP item when inviting Adrestes provides a significant bonus to the reputation earned with him for that week's scenario. Maximize your weekly Infused Ruby gain by successfully catering to the preferences of all four guests, aiming for a final "Excellent" party mood. Your character must be a member of the Venthyr Covenant to host The Ember Court; you can switch covenants in Oribos.

104.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from mobs in the level 35-45 range, found primarily in Classic zones such as Stranglethorn Vale, Desolace, Arathi Highlands, and Dustwallow Marsh. The seven pieces that comprise this green recolor set are the Emblazoned Hat, Emblazoned Shoulders, Emblazoned Vest, Emblazoned Leggings, Emblazoned Gloves, Emblazoned Boots, and Emblazoned Belt. These items are not tied to specific bosses and can drop from any eligible mob in the appropriate level range. The dungeon Razorfen Kraul is also a concentrated source of mobs that can drop these pieces.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout, allowing for continuous farming. The farm is fully soloable at the current maximum level, as all enemies will be trivial. Collecting all pieces is entirely dependent on random drops, which can take anywhere from a few hours to dozens of hours of dedicated farming. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces come from the same general loot table of level 35-45 world mobs, though some pieces may be rarer than others.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE) and are frequently listed for sale. For self-farming, use a high-mobility class with instant area-of-effect abilities, like a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of humanoids or beasts in Stranglethorn Vale. Running the dungeon Razorfen Kraul provides a high density of mobs in an instanced environment, which can be reset up to five times per hour. Any class can farm the BoE items, but only leather-wearing classes can learn the appearance.

105.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon Bind on Equip items, primarily with the "Hibernal" prefix, that drop from various high-level Classic dungeons. The Head piece, Hibernal Helm, drops from Darkmaster Gandling in Scholomance. The Shoulder (Hibernal Mantle), Legs (Hibernal Pants), and Feet (Hibernal Boots) drop from War Master Voone, Mother Smolderweb, and Quartermaster Zigris respectively in Lower Blackrock Spire. The Chest, Hibernal Robe, drops from General Drakkisath in Upper Blackrock Spire. The Hands (Hibernal Gloves) and Waist (Hibernal Belt) drop from High Interrogator Gerstahn and Fineous Darkvire in Blackrock Depths. The Wrist, Hibernal Bracers, is a zone drop from mobs within these same dungeons.

### Farming Logistics
These dungeons have no effective lockout, as they can be reset up to 10 times per hour on Normal difficulty, which is the only difficulty available. All dungeons required for the set (Scholomance, Blackrock Depths, Blackrock Spire) can be fully soloed at current max level. Due to the very large loot tables and low drop chance for specific green items, directly farming could take over 100 runs; purchasing from the Auction House is the more reliable method. This set does not use a token system, but its pieces are scattered across four different instances. There are no separate LFR/Normal/Heroic/Mythic lockouts to consider for Classic-era dungeons.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to obtain this set is to check the Auction House regularly for the "Hibernal" pieces, as all are Bind on Equip and can be bought and sold. When farming directly, use a high-mobility class like a Demon Hunter or Druid to speedrun to the specific bosses and ignore unnecessary trash. In Blackrock Depths, you can use the Mole Machine transport system to skip directly to the required areas. Since the pieces drop from specific bosses, you can kill the target, exit the dungeon, and reset the instance without completing the entire run.

108.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop, primarily found on humanoid and beast mobs between levels 30 and 40. All pieces, including the Gypsy Turban (Head), Gypsy Tunic (Chest), Gypsy Trousers (Legs), Gypsy Gloves (Hands), Gypsy Sandals (Feet), Gypsy Sash (Waist), and Gypsy Wraps (Wrist), are random drops from enemies in this level range. There are no shoulders for this set. Optimal farming zones with high mob density include Stranglethorn Vale, Arathi Highlands, and Desolace.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout or instance reset to consider, allowing for continuous farming. The entire farm is easily soloable at current max level. The number of hours required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to the random nature of world drops. All items in this set are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be traded or sold. There are no tier tokens, and all pieces come from the same general source.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind on Equip and are frequently listed by other players. If farming directly, use a class with high mobility and area-of-effect damage to clear large groups of mobs quickly, such as a Druid or Hunter. Focus on areas with dense humanoid populations, like the troll ruins and pirate camps throughout Stranglethorn Vale. The item names will include random enchantments like "of the Eagle," but the appearance will be collected for your Wardrobe regardless of the suffix.

109.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired from bosses within three separate Classic-era dungeons. Four pieces drop within Wailing Caverns: the Waist from Lady Anacondra, the Hands from Lord Cobrahn, the Wrist from Serpentis, and the Chest from Verdan the Everliving. The Legs drop from Ghamoo-ra in Blackfathom Deeps. The final piece, the Feet, drops from Cookie in The Deadmines. The set does not include a matching Head or Shoulder piece.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons do not have a daily or weekly lockout and can be reset manually after exiting. A single player is limited to entering a maximum of 10 instances per hour across their account. The entire set can be easily soloed by any character at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all pieces directly from bosses should take approximately 5-10 total dungeon runs. This set does not use a tier token system, and pieces drop from three different dungeons, requiring travel between The Cape of Stranglethorn (for The Deadmines), Northern Barrens (for Wailing Caverns), and Ashenvale (for Blackfathom Deeps).

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Grizzly Garb set are Bind on Equip (BoE), so check the Auction House first to purchase them directly, which is often faster than farming. When farming Wailing Caverns, you must defeat all four Serpent Lords (Anacondra, Cobrahn, Serpentis, and Pythas) to summon Verdan the Everliving for the chest piece. Use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to speed up clears between the geographically separated dungeons. There is only one difficulty level for these dungeons, so no specific setting is required.

111.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 20-30 zones and instances from the Classic expansion. There are no specific bosses that drop these items; they can drop from any eligible mob in the appropriate level range. The main slots are Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist. Prime farming zones include Duskwood, Hillsbrad Foothills, Ashenvale, and Stonetalon Mountains. Dungeons such as Razorfen Kraul, Blackfathom Deeps, and Gnomeregan also serve as excellent, high-density sources for these items.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming these pieces in the open world, but dungeon farming is subject to the 10 instances per hour limit. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level with no difficulty. The number of runs required is entirely dependent on random chance, as world drop rates are low and inconsistent. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general loot pool of level 20-30 mobs.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by searching for "Ceremonial" on the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip and are frequently listed by other players. If you prefer to farm it yourself, use a high-mobility class to clear large groups of enemies in the suggested zones or to quickly reset dungeons. For dungeon farming, running Normal Razorfen Kraul is effective due to its high mob density and linear layout. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault are not sources for these items.

112.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Uncommon quality Bind-on-Equip world drops from Classic-era zones. All pieces drop from level 20-30 humanoid mobs, with no specific bosses assigned to any piece. The primary farming zones include Duskwood, Redridge Mountains, Ashenvale, and Stonetalon Mountains. The set includes the Bandit Jerkin (Chest), Bandit Trousers (Legs), Bandit Gloves (Hands), Bandit Boots (Feet), Bandit Cinch (Waist), and Bandit Bracers (Wrist); no matching head or shoulder pieces exist for this specific recolor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as all pieces are farmed from repeatable open-world mobs. The entire farm is fully soloable at maximum level, often with one-shot mechanics against the low-level targets. Due to the extremely low and random drop chance of world drops, the number of "runs" or hours required is highly variable and unpredictable. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces are obtained from the same world drop source pool.

### Farming Tips
The most time-efficient method for obtaining this set is to purchase the pieces directly from the Auction House. Search for each item by name (e.g., "Bandit Jerkin") and be aware that prices can fluctuate significantly. If choosing to farm the items directly, use a max-level character with high AoE damage and movement speed to clear large packs of humanoids in the designated zones. Since the items are Bind-on-Equip, you can farm or purchase them on any character on your realm and mail them to the character who needs the appearance.

114.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop, primarily found on mobs between levels 48 and 60. The most effective farming locations are high-density Classic-era zones like Silithus, Winterspring, and the Eastern Plaguelands. The pieces can also drop from trash mobs inside level 55-60 dungeons such as Scholomance, Stratholme, and Blackrock Spire. As world drops, no specific boss is tied to any specific piece of the set. The set consists of Feral Cover, Feral Shoulderpads, Feral Harness, Feral Leggings, Feral Gloves, Feral Boots, Feral Cord, and Feral Bracers.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as it drops from mobs in open-world zones or from dungeon trash. If farming inside dungeons, you are limited to the standard 10 instances per hour cap. The entire farm can be completed solo by any max-level character. Collection time is highly variable due to the random nature of world drops and can range from a few hours to hundreds of hours of dedicated farming. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces come from the same pool of world drops.

### Farming Tips
All pieces in the Feral Garb set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so the most efficient way to acquire the set is to check the Auction House first. For active farming, create a dedicated route clearing trash mobs inside a Classic dungeon like Stratholme or Blackrock Spire, as the mob density is higher than in open-world zones. Use a character with high mobility and instant area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear trash packs quickly before exiting and resetting the instance.

115.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of uncommon-quality world drops found in the level 1-10 starting zones of the Classic expansion. The primary sources are low-level humanoid mobs such as Kobolds in Elwynn Forest, Defias in Westfall, or Quillboar in Durotar. The set pieces are Frayed Robe (Chest), Frayed Pants (Legs), Frayed Hat (Head), Frayed Mantle (Shoulder), Frayed Gloves (Hands), Frayed Shoes (Feet), Frayed Belt (Waist), and Frayed Bracers (Wrist). There are no specific bosses or dungeons that drop these items; they are random drops from the general mob population in these low-level zones.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is farmed from world mobs that can be killed repeatedly. It is fully soloable by any character, with max-level characters able to eliminate mobs instantly. Due to the low drop chance and large loot tables of these creatures, collecting all eight pieces through farming can take several hours. No tier tokens are involved, and all pieces come from the same general source. Instance difficulties and lockouts are not a factor in this farm.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE) and are often available on the Auction House, which is the most time-efficient acquisition method. If farming directly, use a high-mobility class with area-of-effect abilities to clear densely populated mob areas like the Echoing Deeps mine in Elwynn Forest. Ensure your loot filter is not set to ignore green-quality items. The appearance is learned by any class upon equipping the item, as it is cosmetic cloth with no class restrictions.

116.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "Seer's Garb," an uncommon quality world drop set from various dungeons in the Burning Crusade expansion. The pieces drop from specific bosses within four different instances located in Outland. The Head, Seer's Circlet, drops from Harbinger Skyriss in The Arcatraz. The Shoulder, Seer's Mantle, and Hands, Seer's Gloves, drop from Grand Warlock Nethekurse and Warchief Kargath Bladefist respectively in The Shattered Halls. The Chest, Seer's Robe, and Legs, Seer's Fine-Spun Kilt, drop from Mennu the Betrayer and Quagmirran respectively in Slave Pens. The Feet, Seer's Boots, drop from Hungarfen in The Underbog. No matching Waist or Wrist items exist for this particular set.

### Farming Logistics
The set pieces drop from dungeons that can be run on Normal or Heroic difficulty. Normal difficulty has no lockout and can be reset manually, though you are limited to 10 instance entries per hour across your account. Heroic difficulty has a daily lockout, resetting once per day. The entire set can be easily soloed by a current max-level character. Given the 15-25% drop chance per piece, collecting all six items will likely take 5-15 total runs distributed across the four required dungeons. This set does not use a token system, and Normal and Heroic difficulties share the same loot tables for these items.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest acquisition, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, as this allows you to immediately reset the instance and try again for a specific piece without waiting for a daily lockout. The pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The four dungeons are located in three different zones: Slave Pens and The Underbog are in Coilfang Reservoir (Zangarmarsh), The Shattered Halls is in Hellfire Citadel (Hellfire Peninsula), and The Arcatraz is in Tempest Keep (Netherstorm). Using a high-mobility class will significantly speed up travel time within the linear instances.

117.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Bind on Equip world drops from level 15-25 humanoid mobs in Classic zones. The most concentrated farming location is The Deadmines dungeon in Westfall, as the Defias mobs inside have a high density and frequently drop the pieces. There are no specific bosses for any piece; they all drop from trash mobs. The set consists of Bandit Shoulders, Bandit Jerkin (Chest), Bandit Pants (Legs), Bandit Gloves (Hands), Bandit Cinch (Waist), and Bandit Boots (Feet), with no matching head or wrist items.

### Farming Logistics
Farming inside The Deadmines is subject to the standard dungeon lockout of 10 instances per hour, per account. The farm can be fully soloed at current max level. Due to the low, random drop chance of world drops, collecting the full set could take anywhere from 10 to 50+ dungeon clears or several hours of open-world farming. This set does not use a token system. All pieces are Bind on Equip and drop from the same pool of mobs, with no alternate sources or difficulty-specific lockouts.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the Normal difficulty version of The Deadmines. A max-level character can clear all trash mobs up to the first boss, exit, reset the instance, and repeat the process up to the hourly limit. Since all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), the fastest method to obtain the set is often to purchase the pieces directly from the Auction House. Remember to equip the BoE items on a leather-wearing character to add their appearance to your collection.

118.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop, primarily farmed from humanoid mobs in the level 17-28 range. The best zone to farm all pieces is Duskwood. The Blackened Defias Mask, Gloves, and Boots drop from mobs like Nightbane Worgen, Skeletal Fiends, and Defias Enchanters around level 17-26. The Blackened Defias Armor and Leggings drop from slightly higher-level mobs in the same zone, such as Skeletal Horrors and Defias Night Blades, typically level 21-28. The set does not include matching Shoulders, Waist, or Wrists.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from world mobs, so there is no lockout or instance reset to consider. The farm can be done continuously and is fully soloable at max level for maximum efficiency. Due to the extremely low drop chance for uncommon world drops, collecting the full set can take anywhere from a few hours to dozens of hours of dedicated farming. All pieces drop from humanoid mobs in the same general zones, so no travel between different instance types is required. There are no tier tokens or different difficulty versions for this set.

### Farming Tips
Use a max-level character with high mobility and area-of-effect abilities to clear mobs in Duskwood quickly. All pieces of this set are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can frequently be found on the Auction House, which is the most time-efficient method of acquisition. If farming, focus on high-density humanoid areas like Raven Hill Cemetery or the southern Defias encampments. There are no special skips or lockouts to manage for this farm.

119.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained as a random world drop from humanoid and beast mobs, primarily in the level 20-30 range. All main slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are part of this Bind-on-Equip world drop pool. The most efficient farming zones include Duskwood, the northern part of Stranglethorn Vale, Ashenvale, and Hillsbrad Foothills. There is no specific boss, dungeon, or raid that drops these items; they are found by killing common enemies in the open world.

### Farming Logistics
Farming this set has no lockout, as it is composed of world drops that can be farmed continuously. The entire set can be soloed by any character and is trivial at maximum level. Since drop chances are low and depend on the specific mobs being farmed, collecting all pieces can take a significant amount of time, potentially requiring hundreds or thousands of mob kills. No tier tokens are involved, and all pieces drop from the same general pool of level 20-30 enemies.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by searching for individual pieces on the Auction House, as all items are Bind-on-Equip. For players who prefer to farm it directly, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect abilities to clear large groups of mobs quickly in the target zones. Since the items are uncommon quality, consider using an addon that highlights or auto-loots items of a specific quality to avoid missing a drop. Characters with the Skinning profession can generate additional gold while farming beast-heavy areas for the set.

120.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind on Equip world drops found within the Classic dungeon, Wailing Caverns. The five core pieces drop from specific bosses located inside the instance. The chest, Armor of the Fang, drops from Lord Cobrahn. The legs, Leggings of the Fang, are looted from Lord Pythas. The hands, Gloves of the Fang, drop from Lady Anacondra, and the waist, Belt of the Fang, is found on Lord Serpentis. The final piece, the feet, Footpads of the Fang, is found in the chest that spawns after defeating Mutanus the Devourer.

### Farming Logistics
The instance has a normal dungeon lockout, which can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The entire set can be easily soloed by any max-level character, with a full clear taking only a few minutes. Given the number of pieces and their drop chances, a complete set can typically be farmed in 5-15 runs. There is no tier token system for this set; all loot drops directly from the bosses. Classic dungeons have only one difficulty, so there are no separate lockouts to consider.

### Farming Tips
Since all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), check the Auction House first, as you may be able to purchase the entire set cheaply and avoid farming altogether. When farming, use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to navigate the winding caves of the instance quickly. You must defeat the four required Fang Lords before the final boss can be summoned. There is no way to skip these encounters, so a full clear of all relevant bosses is necessary on each run.

121.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from level 48-58 mobs throughout Classic Azeroth zones. There are no specific bosses that drop the pieces; they are found on the loot tables of creatures within the appropriate level range. Key farming zones include Western Plaguelands, Eastern Plaguelands, Felwood, and Burning Steppes. The complete set consists of the Emblazoned Hat, Emblazoned Shoulders, Emblazoned Robe, Emblazoned Pants, Emblazoned Gloves, Emblazoned Boots, Emblazoned Belt, and Emblazoned Bracers. Any mob within the level range in these zones has a small chance to drop a piece.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout or instance reset to consider, allowing for continuous farming. The set is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low drop chance for specific world drops, the time required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can take many hours of farming. There is no tier token system for this set, and all pieces come from the same world drop loot pool. The concept of separate raid or dungeon difficulties does not apply to this farm.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip (BoE). Farming should be done with a high-mobility class capable of large area-of-effect pulls to maximize kills per hour. Focus on areas with high humanoid mob density, such as the undead scourge in the Plaguelands, for the best chance at cloth armor drops. Remember that you must equip a BoE item on a character that can wear that armor type for it to be added to your wardrobe.

123.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of uncommon quality Bind-on-Equip world drops, primarily found in The Deadmines dungeon and from Defias-affiliated mobs in the zones of Westfall and Duskwood. The chest piece, Blackened Defias Armor, drops from mobs in Duskwood and bosses like Cookie in The Deadmines. The legs, Blackened Defias Leggings, also drop in Duskwood from mobs such as Stitches. The hands, Blackened Defias Gloves, and feet, Blackened Defias Boots, are commonly found on mobs in Westfall and drop from The Deadmines bosses Miner Johnson and Sneed, respectively. The waist piece, Blackened Defias Belt, drops from mobs in Westfall and the boss Rhahk'Zor inside the dungeon. There are no matching head, shoulder, or wrist pieces for this specific recolor set.

### Farming Logistics
Farming inside The Deadmines is subject to the standard dungeon lockout: players below level 60 can reset the instance up to 10 times per hour, while higher-level characters are limited to a daily reset. The outdoor world drop farm has no lockout. The set can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Due to low drop chances, collecting the full set can take between 20 to 100 dungeon runs or equivalent time spent farming mobs. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same type of content. Only the "Classic" Normal difficulty version of The Deadmines contains these items, as the Cataclysm Heroic version has a different loot table.

### Farming Tips
For the most efficient farming, use a high-level character to clear the Normal difficulty "Classic" version of The Deadmines. Because all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), they can be purchased directly from the Auction House, which is often the fastest method of acquisition. Any class can farm the items, but only a Leather-wearing class (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter) can equip them to add the appearances to the account-wide wardrobe. When farming, focus on full-clearing The Deadmines or using a class with high mobility and area-of-effect damage to quickly eliminate mobs in Westfall or Duskwood.

124.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind on Equip (BoE) world drops primarily found in level 25-35 Classic dungeons. The best instances to farm for these pieces are Razorfen Kraul and Blackfathom Deeps due to the high density of mobs in the appropriate level range. All pieces share the "Headhunter's" name prefix and drop from trash mobs and bosses within these zones. The specific slots are Headhunter's Head-covering (Head), Headhunter's Shoulders (Shoulder), Headhunter's Tunic (Chest), Headhunter's Trousers (Legs), Headhunter's Mitts (Hands), Headhunter's Boots (Feet), Headhunter's Belt (Waist), and Headhunter's Bands (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no lockout, but you are limited to 10 instance resets per hour on a single character. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the random nature of world drops, the number of runs required is highly variable and could range from a few dozen to several hundred dungeon clears. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general loot tables within the target zones. Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties do not apply to these Classic dungeons.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to move through the instance quickly. Since all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), the most efficient way to obtain the set is often to purchase the items directly from the Auction House. When farming inside dungeons, prioritize killing all trash mob packs, as they have the highest cumulative chance of dropping the pieces compared to the bosses. There are no skips required; a full clear of an instance like Razorfen Kraul is the most effective strategy.

125.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a cloth world drop from Classic zones, primarily from humanoid, demon, and undead mobs in the level 35-45 range. The best zones for farming are Stranglethorn Vale, Dustwallow Marsh, Desolace, and Arathi Highlands. Unlike instance sets, specific pieces do not drop from specific bosses. The main armor pieces are Nocturnal Cap (Head), Nocturnal Shoulder Pads (Shoulder), Nocturnal Robe (Chest), Nocturnal Leggings (Legs), Nocturnal Gloves (Hands), and Nocturnal Slippers (Feet), all of which can drop from any eligible mob within the level range.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is farmed from respawning mobs in outdoor zones. The farm is fully soloable by any max-level character, and it is trivial in terms of difficulty. The time required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to the low, random nature of world drops, potentially taking dozens of hours. The set does not use a token system, and all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE). There are no different difficulties or lockouts to consider.

### Farming Tips
The fastest and most practical way to obtain this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip and commonly available. If you choose to farm it yourself, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs efficiently. Focus on high-density areas like the troll ruins in Stranglethorn Vale or the naga encampments along the coast of Desolace. No special skips, raid tools, or group mechanics are relevant to this farm.

126.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Looking for Raid difficulty plate armor appearance from the Sanctum of Domination raid in Shadowlands. The individual pieces are obtained from specific bosses within the LFR version of the instance. The Head drops from Painsmith Raznal, and the Shoulder drops from Kel'Thuzad. The Chest is looted from Fatescribe Roh-Kalo, while the Legs drop from the Guardian of the First Ones. The Hands are a drop from Soulrender Dormazain, the Feet from Remnant of Ner'zhul, and both the Waist and Wrist pieces drop from The Tarragrue.

### Farming Logistics
The Sanctum of Domination raid has a weekly lockout per character for each difficulty. The LFR difficulty required for this set has its own separate weekly lockout from Normal, Heroic, and Mythic. The entire raid is soloable by a max-level character. This set is comprised of leather armor pieces, so it must be farmed on a Demon Hunter, Druid, Monk, or Rogue to be equipped and added to your wardrobe. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting all eight pieces may take between 5-10 full clears on a single character. This raid does not use a tier token system; gear drops directly as individual items from bosses.

### Farming Tips
To farm the LFR version specifically, visit the NPC Ta'elfar in Oribos (in the Hall of Shapes) and choose the "Sanctum of Domination" LFR wings from his dialogue options. You can queue for the four wings individually, allowing you to target only the bosses you need if you have already collected some pieces. All items in this set are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Using a class with high mobility, such as a Demon Hunter or Monk, will speed up the process of moving between bosses within the raid wings.

128.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality world drops, primarily found on mobs between levels 42 and 52. The best locations for farming are the dungeons Zul'Farrak and Maraudon, or the open-world zones of Tanaris, Feralas, and The Hinterlands. The items are not tied to specific bosses and can drop from any eligible mob in these locations. The set pieces are: Head (Watcher's Cap), Shoulder (Watcher's Mantle), Chest (Watcher's Tunic), Legs (Watcher's Leggings), Hands (Watcher's Gloves), Feet (Watcher's Boots), Waist (Watcher's Cinch), and Wrist (Watcher's Bracers).

### Farming Logistics
This farm has no lockout; dungeons can be reset up to 10 times per hour, and open-world mobs can be farmed continuously. The entire set can be easily soloed by any character at current max level. Due to the random nature of world drops, the time required to collect all pieces can vary from a few hours to dozens of hours. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general loot pool. Classic-era zones and dungeons do not have different difficulties like LFR, Normal, or Heroic.

### Farming Tips
Check the Auction House first, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip and can often be purchased directly, saving significant time. If farming manually, running the dungeon Zul'Farrak is highly efficient due to its high density of humanoid mobs in the required level range. A max-level character can clear the entire instance in minutes, exit, reset, and repeat. Since these are world drops, mechanics like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply.

129.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a random world drop from mobs and chests in the level 15-25 range. The best zones for farming include The Barrens, Darkshore, Silverpine Forest, and Westfall due to their high density of appropriate-level mobs. All pieces, including the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist, share this same drop source. They can also be found inside low-level dungeons such as Deadmines, Wailing Caverns, and Shadowfang Keep. The items do not drop from specific bosses but rather from any eligible mob within the level bracket.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as it is a world drop. The farm is completely soloable by any character significantly above level 25. Due to the low and random nature of world drops, the time required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can take many hours of dedicated farming. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces are obtained from the same sources. There are no different difficulties or lockouts to consider for this type of farm.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by searching for individual pieces on the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip (BoE). When searching, look for the base name (e.g., "Scouting Tunic") and ignore the random suffix ("of the Tiger," etc.), as the appearance is the same. If farming directly, use a high-mobility class with area-of-effect abilities to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Farming low-level dungeons can be more efficient than the open world because of the higher mob density in a confined area.

131.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is obtained by purchasing the "Ensemble: Garb of the Twilight Grove" from the Night Fae Covenant vendor in Ardenweald. The vendor, Elwyn, is the Renown Quartermaster located in the Heart of the Forest. The ensemble costs 10,000 Reservoir Anima. Purchasing and learning the ensemble requires being a member of the Night Fae Covenant, reaching Renown 60, and completing Chapter 3 of the 9.1 Chains of Domination campaign ("Focusing the Eye"). The single ensemble item unlocks all eight appearances for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Back slots.

### Farming Logistics
This set is purchased with currency, so it has no lockout and can be farmed continuously. All required content for farming Reservoir Anima is easily soloable at the current maximum level. The time needed to collect the 10,000 Anima can range from a few hours to a couple of days, depending on farming efficiency. Anima is rewarded from Shadowlands world quests, treasures, rare elites, dungeon quests, and the command table. Although you must be a member of the Night Fae covenant to purchase the item, once the ensemble is learned, the appearances are available account-wide to all characters of any class or covenant.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Reservoir Anima is by looting treasures and killing rare elites in Zereth Mortis, as they provide large amounts of currency. If you have a character with high Renown in a different covenant, you can farm the Anima on that character and then swap to the Night Fae covenant in Oribos to make the purchase, a process which is free and instant once you have reached Renown 80 on any character. The ensemble item is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased on the Auction House. Remember that the Chains of Domination campaign quest is a mandatory prerequisite and is started by accepting "The First Move" in Oribos.

132.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Raid Finder difficulty tier set from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands. The five main tier pieces drop from specific bosses: the Hands drop from Lihuvim, Principal Architect; the Legs from Halondrus the Reclaimer; the Head from Anduin Wrynn; the Shoulders from the Lords of Dread; and the Chest from Rygelon. The non-tier lookalike Waist drops from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, the Feet drop from Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener, and the Wrists drop from Anduin Wrynn. All pieces are from the same raid instance on Raid Finder difficulty.

### Farming Logistics
This set is on a weekly raid lockout per boss. At the current max level, the entire Raid Finder version of Sepulcher of the First Ones can be easily soloed by any of the eligible classes. Due to the weekly lockout and number of items, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 4-8 weeks. The tier pieces are obtained via the Mystic token, which is shared by Hunters, Mages, and Shamans; the appearance will also unlock for Evokers. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts, but this specific green recolor only drops in Raid Finder.

### Farming Tips
To access the Raid Finder version, speak to the NPC Ta'elfar in Oribos, located at coordinates 64, 21. Tier tokens must be right-clicked in your inventory by an eligible class (Hunter or Shaman) to create the item and add the appearance to your collection. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The Great Vault and bonus roll systems from Shadowlands are no longer active for this content, so drops are entirely dependent on personal loot from boss kills.

133.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from level 47-57 mobs throughout Azeroth. All eight pieces—Insignia Cap (Head), Insignia Mantle (Shoulder), Insignia Robes (Chest), Insignia Leggings (Legs), Insignia Gloves (Hands), Insignia Boots (Feet), Insignia Sash (Waist), and Insignia Bracers (Wrist)—share the same random drop chance from this loot table. No specific boss drops any particular piece. The most effective farming locations are dungeons with high mob density, such as Blackrock Depths, Stratholme, and Scholomance, or open-world zones like Winterspring and the Eastern Plaguelands.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout when farming mobs in the open world. If farming inside dungeons, you are subject to the cap of 10 normal-difficulty instance resets per hour. This entire farm is easily soloable by any class at the current maximum level. This set does not use a tier token system, as it is a collection of random world drops. All pieces drop from the same mob level range, so no specific location needs to be targeted for an individual slot. Due to the extremely low probability of specific world drops, collecting the full set through targeted farming is highly variable and can take a significant amount of time.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House. All items in the set are Bind on Equip (BoE) and are frequently listed for sale by other players. If you choose to farm the set yourself, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect abilities to clear large packs of mobs quickly. Focus on high-density areas, such as the initial chambers of Blackrock Depths, and run the dungeon on Normal difficulty to allow for repeated resets.

134.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from the Kyrian Covenant vendor, Adjutant Galos, in Elysian Hold, Bastion. The full set costs 9,000 Reservoir Anima and requires reaching Renown 60 with the Kyrian Covenant. The pieces and their individual costs are: Ghostwalker's Diadem (Head, 1000 Anima), Ghostwalker's Pauldrons (Shoulder, 1000 Anima), Ghostwalker's Wraps (Chest, 1500 Anima), Ghostwalker's Leggings (Legs, 1500 Anima), Ghostwalker's Gauntlets (Hands, 1000 Anima), Ghostwalker's Treads (Feet, 1000 Anima), Ghostwalker's Cord (Waist, 1000 Anima), and Ghostwalker's Armbands (Wrist, 1000 Anima). The back slot item, Ghostwalker's Drape, is a separate purchase for 1,250 Anima.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set as it is purchased with a currency. The acquisition is gated by your ability to farm Reservoir Anima and the one-time requirement of reaching Renown 60 with the Kyrian Covenant. Farming the required 9,000 Anima can be done entirely solo and the time it takes depends on your farming efficiency. All pieces are from the same vendor and source. The appearance is cosmetic and can be learned by any class, but only a character aligned with the Kyrian Covenant can purchase the items.

### Farming Tips
To farm Reservoir Anima efficiently, focus on completing World Quests in all four Shadowlands zones, especially those that award 250 or 500 Anima. Daily Covenant Callings and weekly quests like "Replenish the Reservoir" provide large amounts of Anima. Farming rare mobs and treasures in Zereth Mortis is also a highly effective method. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. Once purchased, the appearance is unlocked account-wide for all characters regardless of their armor type or Covenant allegiance.

135.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from world drops within the Shadowlands zone of Revendreth. All pieces of the set, including the Archer's Cowl (Head), Spaulders (Shoulder), Tunic (Chest), Leggings (Legs), Handwraps (Hands), Slippers (Feet), Cinch (Waist), and Bracers (Wrist), can drop from any standard, non-elite creature found throughout the zone. There are no specific bosses or locations tied to individual pieces. The entire set is Bind-on-Equip, meaning it can also be purchased from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it relies on killing respawning world mobs. The farm is fully soloable at the current maximum level, as Shadowlands creatures pose no threat. The time required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to random world drop chances; it can take anywhere from one hour to many hours of continuous farming. No tier token system is used for this set, and all pieces drop from the same type of source.

### Farming Tips
Before farming, check the Auction House, as individual pieces are often listed for sale and can be inexpensive. For manual farming, a good location is Wanecrypt Hill in northwestern Revendreth due to its high density of humanoid mobs that respawn quickly. Use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Hunter, to kill large groups of enemies efficiently. The set does not require a specific Covenant or questline to be eligible to drop.

137.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the base cosmetic armor from the Night Fae Covenant, awarded for completing chapters of the covenant's campaign in Shadowlands. The specific pieces are guaranteed rewards from the following quests which conclude each chapter.
*   **Head & Shoulder:** Awarded from "A New Beginning," the final quest of Chapter 8.
*   **Chest:** Awarded from "The Fading of the Vale," the final quest of Chapter 7.
*   **Legs:** Awarded from "The Night's Gambit," the final quest of Chapter 5.
*   **Hands & Feet:** Awarded from "The Horn of the Wild Hunt," the final quest of Chapter 3.
*   **Waist & Wrist:** Awarded from "Drust to Drust," the final quest of Chapter 2.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set as it is obtained through a one-time questline. The entire Shadowlands covenant campaign is fully soloable at current max level. Obtaining the full set is guaranteed upon completion of the first eight chapters of the campaign and does not rely on random drops. This set does not use a token system. Once you collect all pieces on a single character, the full cosmetic set is added to your account-wide wardrobe, usable by any character regardless of armor type.

### Farming Tips
To begin the Night Fae campaign, you must first pledge allegiance to the covenant in Oribos. If you have already completed the main Shadowlands story on another character, you can use the Threads of Fate skip to immediately choose a covenant and begin its campaign. All pieces are soulbound quest rewards and cannot be purchased on the Auction House. Should you delete a piece, it can be re-purchased from Elwyn, the Renown Quartermaster in the Heart of the Forest, Ardenweald.

139.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from the Classic expansion, found on mobs roughly between levels 40 and 50. The best zones for farming include Tanaris, Feralas, The Hinterlands, and the Searing Gorge. Specific dungeons such as Zul'Farrak, Maraudon, and the lower sections of Blackrock Depths are also excellent sources due to their high mob density. All main armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are random drops from any humanoid, demon, or undead mob within this level range and are not tied to specific bosses.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it relies on killing respawning world mobs or resetting dungeons. The dungeon instance limit is 10 instances per hour per account. The set is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to its nature as a random world drop, the time required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can range from a few hours to several weeks of dedicated farming. No tier token system is used, and all pieces are obtained from the same pool of world drops.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and are frequently listed for sale. If you prefer to farm it directly, the dungeon Zul'Farrak in Tanaris is a superior location; pulling large groups of trolls, especially during the zombie graveyard event, maximizes kills per hour. A fast-moving class is recommended to clear the zone or instance quickly. Any character that can equip cloth armor can bind the items to their character to learn the appearance for all characters on the account.

140.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 35-45 mobs in original Classic zones. All pieces are Bind on Equip, making the Auction House the most reliable acquisition source. If farming directly, the best zones include Dustwallow Marsh, the Cape of Stranglethorn, Arathi Highlands, and the Scarlet Monastery dungeon. The core armor pieces are random drops from any mob within the appropriate level range, with no specific bosses assigned to any slot. The main armor slots are the Nocturnal Cap (Head), Nocturnal Shoulder Pads (Shoulder), Nocturnal Tunic (Chest), Nocturnal Wristbands (Wrist), Nocturnal Gloves (Hands), Nocturnal Sash (Waist), Nocturnal Leggings (Legs), and Nocturnal Moccasins (Feet).

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is comprised of world drops from outdoor zones and dungeons that can be reset. It is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drops, the number of runs required is unpredictable and could range from dozens to thousands of hours of farming. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces originate from the same pool of world drops, though their individual drop rates may vary slightly.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House. Farming for these items directly is highly time-consuming and not recommended due to the low drop chance. If you choose to farm, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly in high-density areas. Running an instance like Scarlet Monastery repeatedly can be more effective than world farming due to the high number of enemies in a confined, instanced space that can be reset.

141.content:
### How to Obtain
This cloth armor set is a random world drop obtained from mobs in the level 40-50 range, primarily within Classic dungeons. All pieces of the set, such as the Watcher's Cap, Robes, and Mantle, can drop from any non-boss creature in the appropriate level range. The most efficient farming locations are dungeons with high mob density, specifically Uldaman, Zul'Farrak, and Maraudon. No specific bosses are tied to these drops; the entire set comes from the shared loot table of trash mobs.

### Farming Logistics
The farm uses the standard dungeon system, which has no lockout and allows for up to 10 instance resets per hour on Normal difficulty. The set can be fully soloed at current max level, with each run taking only a few minutes to clear the relevant trash mobs. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance for any given piece per full clear, collecting all eight pieces would take approximately 30-50 runs. This set does not use a tier token system, and there are no alternate sources for any of the pieces. Classic dungeons only have a single difficulty, so multiple lockouts are not a factor.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so the most reliable and fastest method of acquisition is often to purchase them from the Auction House. When farming in a dungeon like Uldaman, do not kill any bosses or complete the instance; focus solely on clearing large packs of trash mobs, then exit and reset. Use a character with high mobility and area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to maximize the number of mobs killed per hour. There are no skips or special mechanics involved beyond killing trash as quickly as possible.

145.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from mobs between levels 35 and 45. All eight pieces—Nocturnal Cap, Nocturnal Shoulder Pads, Nocturnal Tunic, Nocturnal Leggings, Nocturnal Gloves, Nocturnal Boots, Nocturnal Belt, and Nocturnal Wristbands—are random drops from this loot pool. The most effective zones for farming these mobs are Stranglethorn Vale, Arathi Highlands, and Desolace. There are no specific bosses or quests that award these items; they are found exclusively through killing creatures in the open world within the appropriate level range.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as it is composed entirely of world drops. The farm can be completed entirely solo at maximum level, as the mobs are low-level and pose no threat. Due to the extremely large loot tables and low drop chances for specific green items, directly farming the complete set could take dozens of hours of continuous mob grinding. No tier tokens are involved, and all pieces drop from the same general source. All pieces are Bind-on-Equip, meaning they can be traded or sold on the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House; search for "Nocturnal" and filter by the appropriate armor type. If you choose to farm it directly, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on areas with high mob density to maximize kills per hour. Since the items are BoE, check the Auction House periodically even while farming, as individual pieces may be listed for a low price.

147.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from uncommon items found in level 35-45 Classic dungeons, primarily Scarlet Monastery, Uldaman, and Razorfen Downs. The chest piece, Watcher's Tunic, drops from Arcanist Doan in Scarlet Monastery: Library, while the wrist, Watcher's Bindings, drops from Houndmaster Loksey in Scarlet Monastery: Armory. The shoulder, hands, and feet pieces are zone drops primarily found by farming trash mobs within Uldaman. The waist is a zone drop from Scarlet Monastery, and the legs are a zone drop from Razorfen Downs. The head piece is the Shadoweave Mask, a crafted item from the Tailoring profession.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons for this set have no daily or weekly lockout and can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The entire farm is fully soloable at maximum level, with all bosses being defeated in a single hit. The boss-specific pieces will take approximately 4-7 runs per item to acquire, based on a 15-25% drop chance. The numerous zone-drop pieces have a significantly lower drop rate and may require many more full clears of their respective dungeons. No tier tokens are associated with this set, and the head piece is crafted by Tailors, not a dungeon drop. There are no separate LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic difficulties for these classic dungeon drops.

### Farming Tips
All of the zone-drop pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can frequently be purchased directly from the Auction House, which is the most efficient method of acquisition. For pieces that must be farmed, use a high-mobility class to clear the dungeons quickly; a character with a speed-increasing talent build is effective for trash farming. When farming boss-specific items in Scarlet Monastery, you can run directly to the final bosses of the Library and Armory wings to save time. The crafted head piece can either be made by a character with Classic Tailoring or purchased from another player or the Auction House.

151.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 51-60 zones and instances introduced in Classic. The most effective farming locations are the dungeons Scholomance and Stratholme due to their high density of elite mobs. All pieces are random drops from trash mobs and bosses within these zones. The main armor slots are the Cabalist Hood (Head), Cabalist Shoulders (Shoulder), Cabalist Robes (Chest), Cabalist Leggings (Legs), Cabalist Gloves (Hands), Cabalist Boots (Feet), Cabalist Belt (Waist), and Cabalist Bracers (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no lockout, as you will run the Normal difficulty of the dungeons, which can be reset manually. However, you are limited by the cap of 10 instances per hour per account. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. The number of runs required is highly variable due to the low, random drop chance of world drops; it can take anywhere from a few dozen to several hundred clears. There is no tier token system for this set, and the pieces drop individually.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Cabalist Garb set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so the fastest way to acquire the set is often by purchasing it from the Auction House. If farming manually, use a class with high mobility and area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, to clear the instances quickly. To maximize your chances per run, kill all trash mobs in the dungeon rather than just rushing to the bosses. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault do not apply to loot from these dungeons.

152.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted by the Leatherworking profession using materials and patterns primarily found within the Wailing Caverns dungeon in The Barrens. The main reagent is Deviate Scale, which drops from Deviate Ravagers, Deviate Guardians, and other beasts inside the instance. The three patterns needed to craft the set also drop from various mobs within Wailing Caverns. The specific items that comprise this set are the Deviate Scale Breastplate, Deviate Scale Belt, and Deviate Scale Gloves. There are no associated head, shoulder, leg, feet, or wrist items in the named set.

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no daily or weekly lockout, but is subject to the retail client's limit of 10 instances per hour per account. The Wailing Caverns dungeon can be fully soloed at maximum level by any class. Acquiring all necessary patterns and materials typically takes 5-10 full clears of the dungeon's non-boss mobs. This set does not use a tier token system, as it is a crafted set rather than a raid tier set. All patterns and materials originate from the same source, Wailing Caverns, and there are no alternate difficulties for this classic dungeon.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to check the Auction House first, as both the patterns and the finished armor pieces are Bind-on-Equip and frequently available for purchase. If farming, focus on clearing the non-boss trash mobs in the main circular cavern, as they are the primary source of both Deviate Scales and the patterns. It is not necessary to kill any of the bosses. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault do not apply to this farm, as it is legacy dungeon content.

153.content:
### How to Obtain
This cloth set is a world drop primarily found within the level 58-60 dungeons of Scholomance and Stratholme in the Eastern Plaguelands. While some pieces are random zone drops within these instances, others have a high chance to drop from specific bosses. Key drops include the Necropile Helm from Darkmaster Gandling in Scholomance, Necropile Robe from various bosses with a notable drop chance from Instructor Malicia in Scholomance, and Necropile Cuffs from The Unforgiven in Stratholme. The matching Shadoweave Shoulders are crafted by Tailors but the pattern is no longer obtainable; the closest visual match that drops is the Boreal Mantle from mobs in the dungeons. The remaining pieces (Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist) drop from various bosses and trash mobs throughout both Scholomance and Stratholme.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is subject to the standard normal dungeon lockout of 10 instances per hour, per account. The instances can be reset manually after each clear by right-clicking your character portrait. This entire set is easily soloable by any max-level character, with full clears of either dungeon taking only a few minutes. Due to the random nature of the drops, collecting the full set can take anywhere from 5 to 20 runs, heavily dependent on luck with the zone-wide drops. There is no tier token system for this set, and the pieces are collected individually. The appearance is only collectable by cloth-wearing classes: Mage, Priest, and Warlock.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, perform full clears of Scholomance, killing all bosses and trash mobs to maximize your chances at the Bind on Equip (BoE) world drops. Since all pieces of this set are BoE, the fastest way to acquire them is often to check the Auction House, as other players may sell the pieces they find. There are no relevant skips for this farm, as a full clear is optimal. Running on Normal difficulty is sufficient, as Heroic difficulty is only available during Timewalking events and does not increase the drop chance for these specific items.

154.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-set cloth items that drop from final bosses in various Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons. The Head, Cowl of the Grand Engineer, drops from Mechano-Lord Capacitus and the Legs, Trousers of the Astromancer, drop from Grand Astromancer Capernian in Heroic The Mechanar. The Shoulder, Mana-Sphere Shoulderguards, drops from High Botanist Freywinn and the Chest, Robe of the Great Dark Beyond, drops from Warp Splinter in Heroic The Botanica. The Hands, Gloves of the Fallen Wizard, drop from Warchief Kargath Bladefist in Heroic The Shattered Halls. The Feet, Boots of the Incorrupt, drop from Quagmirran in Heroic The Slave Pens, while the Waist and Wrist drop in Heroic Shadow Labyrinth: Evoker's Silk Cord from Grandmaster Vorpil and Cuffs of the Devastator from Murmur.

### Farming Logistics
The five required dungeons are on a daily dungeon reset for Heroic difficulty. All instances can be easily soloed by a current max-level character. Assuming an average drop chance, obtaining the full 8-piece set will likely require 5-10 resets per dungeon, spread across roughly one to two weeks of daily farming. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct drops from their respective bosses. All pieces drop exclusively from Heroic difficulty, which has a separate daily lockout from the repeatable Normal difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering each instance. Using a high-mobility class such as a Monk, Druid, or Demon Hunter will significantly speed up travel time within the instances. To minimize travel time between dungeons, set a hearthstone in Shattrath City for quick access to the Tempest Keep and Auchindoun instance portals. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

155.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon-quality world drops, primarily found on humanoid mobs between levels 41-51. The main pieces are the Cadaverous Headguard, Shoulderpads, Armor (Chest), Leggings, Gloves, and Walkers (Feet), with a matching Cadaverous Belt and Bracers (Wrist). The most concentrated source of eligible mobs is inside dungeons. The most effective dungeons for farming these drops are Zul'Farrak in Tanaris and Uldaman in the Badlands, due to their high density of humanoid targets within the correct level range.

### Farming Logistics
Dungeon farming is limited by the 10 instances per hour, per account cap; there is no daily or weekly lockout. The entire set is easily soloed by any class at current max level. Due to the large loot tables and low drop chances for any specific world drop, collecting the full set via farming can take hundreds of runs; there is no tier token system. All pieces drop from the same general loot tables and are not tied to specific difficulties, as these dungeons only have one mode.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, use a high-mobility class to clear large groups of non-elite mobs and reset the dungeon. The most practical way to acquire this set is by purchasing the Bind-on-Equip (BoE) pieces from the Auction House, as targeted farming is highly inefficient. Check the Auction House regularly for pieces, as prices can vary significantly. While any class can farm the BoE items to sell, only Leather-specialization classes (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter) can learn the appearances.

159.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of cloth armor pieces that drop from various final bosses in Level 70 Heroic dungeons from The Burning Crusade. The Head, Cowl of the Grand Engineer, drops from Mekgineer Steamrigger in Heroic The Steamvault. The Shoulder, Mantle of the Mind Flayer, is from Murmur in Heroic Shadow Labyrinth. Find the Chest, Robe of the Great Dark Beyond, on Nexus-Prince Shaffar in Heroic Mana-Tombs, and the Legs, Leggings of the Ethereal, on Warp Splinter in Heroic The Botanica. The Hands, Gloves of the Nether-Stalker, drop from Pathaleon the Calculator in Heroic The Mechanar, while the Feet, Boots of the Incorporeal, are from Warchief Kargath Bladefist in Heroic The Shattered Halls. The Waist, Sash of the Ethereal, drops from The Black Stalker in Heroic The Underbog, and the Wrist, Bracers of the Incorporeal, drops from Harbinger Skyriss in Heroic The Arcatraz.

### Farming Logistics
The set is subject to a daily dungeon reset, as all pieces drop from Heroic difficulty dungeons which lock you out for 24 hours after completion. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level. Due to each piece dropping from a different instance, you have one chance per day for each specific slot. With an approximate 20% drop chance per item, acquiring the full 8-piece set can take several weeks of consistent daily runs across all eight dungeons. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces are direct boss drops and are not class-restricted for collection.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your dungeon difficulty is set to Heroic before entering each instance. At max level, all trash and boss mechanics can be ignored, allowing you to run directly to the target boss for a quick kill. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. While most dungeons are straightforward, access to The Arcatraz requires either completing the lengthy attunement questline or having a character with high-level lockpicking or engineering to open the door.

161.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted through the Abomination Factory, the sanctum feature specific to the Necrolord Covenant in Shadowlands. Players must upgrade their Abomination Factory to unlock the patterns, which are then crafted at the Stitching Table. The primary reagent for all pieces is Malleable Flesh, a currency earned from the weekly Abomination Factory quest and other activities in Maldraxxus. The specific crafted items are: Visage of the Stitcher (Head), Pauldrons of the Stitcher (Shoulder), Jerkin of the Stitcher (Chest), Leggings of the Stitcher (Legs), Handwraps of the Stitcher (Hands), Boots of the Stitcher (Feet), Girdle of the Stitcher (Waist), and Bracers of the Stitcher (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
The farm is not subject to a traditional raid or dungeon lockout but is time-gated by the acquisition of Malleable Flesh and Superior Parts from daily and weekly quests. The entire process is designed to be completed solo. Collecting the full set requires upgrading the Abomination Factory through multiple ranks, which can take several weeks of consistent play to gather the necessary Anima and Redeemed Souls. No tier tokens are involved, and all pieces are crafted from the same source. This is a covenant-specific activity, so the character crafting the set must be a member of the Necrolord Covenant.

### Farming Tips
To accelerate the process, prioritize upgrading the Abomination Factory to Rank 5 to unlock all patterns and quests. Focus on completing the weekly quest "Putting A Plan Together" from Abomination-Attendant Phrobus, as it is the largest source of Malleable Flesh. Additionally, complete the daily quests offered by your constructed abominations, as they also reward the necessary crafting materials. The crafted pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be sold on the Auction House, but once an appearance is learned, it becomes available account-wide for all characters.

162.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Uncommon (green) quality Bind-on-Equip world drops found in Burning Crusade content. All pieces are random drops from trash mobs inside dungeons intended for levels 60-65, with no specific boss dropping any particular piece. The eight items are the Scouting Turban (Head), Scouting Spaulders (Shoulder), Scouting Tunic (Chest), Scouting Trousers (Legs), Scouting Gloves (Hands), Scouting Boots (Feet), Scouting Belt (Waist), and Scouting Bracers (Wrist). The most efficient farming locations are the dense trash packs within Normal difficulty Hellfire Citadel dungeons like The Blood Furnace or Coilfang Reservoir dungeons like The Slave Pens.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed in Normal difficulty dungeons, which have no lockout and can be reset up to 10 times per hour per account. The farm is fully soloable at current max level, as all enemies will be defeated in a single hit. Due to the extremely low and random drop chance for specific world drops, collecting the full set can take anywhere from 50 to over 200 instance clears. This set does not use a tier token system, as it is not a raid tier set. While pieces can drop from outdoor mobs in zones like Hellfire Peninsula, farming instanced trash is significantly more efficient.

### Farming Tips
Always run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, as this allows you to exit, reset the instance, and re-enter immediately. The optimal farming strategy is to clear all trash mobs up to the first boss in a dungeon like The Blood Furnace, then leave the instance and reset it without killing any bosses. All pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so the fastest way to complete the set is to check the Auction House frequently, as other players may sell the pieces they find. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid with Feral Affinity or a Monk, will substantially decrease the time spent on each run.

163.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is obtained from the Kyrian Covenant's Path of Ascension feature in Shadowlands. The appearance is purchased as two separate ensembles from Forgelite Neptira in Elysian Hold, Bastion, using Medallions of Service as currency. The "Ensemble: Steward's Attire" contains the Shoulder and Chest pieces, costs 1,500 Medallions of Service, and requires defeating the bosses Kalisthene and Echthra on Humility difficulty. The "Ensemble: Steward's Regalia" contains the Head, Hands, Waist, and Feet pieces, costs 2,500 Medallions of Service, and requires defeating Myn'ir, Alderyn & Myn'ir, Nuuminuuru, and Craven Corinth on Humility difficulty. Wrists and Legs are not part of this set, as the leg appearance is built into the robe.

### Farming Logistics
The Path of Ascension is a solo-player scenario and has no daily or weekly lockout; attempts are limited only by your ability to craft the required entry keys. The entire feature is easily soloed at current max level. The primary time investment comes from gathering the 4,000 total Medallions of Service, which can take several weeks of completing Bastion world quests and Covenant callings. This farm must be done on a character who is a member of the Kyrian Covenant. Unlocking the ensembles is character-specific, but once learned, the appearance is available account-wide.

### Farming Tips
All required boss victories are on the "Humility" difficulty, which is the easiest setting. To clear the trials faster, focus your Anima on upgrading the Soulbind Pelagos and his associated equipment, as he is the most effective combatant for the encounters. The ensembles are Bind-on-Account, meaning you only need to complete the farm on one Kyrian character to unlock the appearances for all characters on your account. Stockpile the key-crafting materials, such as Elysian Feathers, to ensure you can make attempts whenever needed without pausing to farm reagents.

164.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Normal difficulty cloth armor from Shadowlands dungeons. Each piece drops from a specific dungeon boss. The Head, Shoulder, and Wrist pieces drop from Grand Vizier in The Necrotic Wake. The Chest is from Dealer Xy'exa in De Other Side. The Legs and Feet are from Tred'ova in Mists of Tirna Scithe. Finally, the Hands and Waist drop from Executor Tarvold in Sanguine Depths.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from Normal difficulty dungeons, which have no daily or weekly lockout but are subject to the account-wide limit of 10 instances per hour. At the current maximum level, all Shadowlands dungeons are easily soloable by any class. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance per item, it may take 10-20 total targeted dungeon runs to collect all eight pieces. This set does not use a token system; loot drops directly for your armor type. Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate appearances and do not share lockouts.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Normal before entering to ensure you are farming the correct appearance. Use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, to run through the dungeons quickly, skipping all non-essential trash mobs. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. The Great Vault and bonus roll systems are not applicable for farming this specific Normal-difficulty dungeon set.

165.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted through the Leatherworking profession with Classic-era recipes requiring a skill of 205-250. The patterns are obtained from a mix of trainers, vendors, and world drops. The head, shoulders, hands, and feet patterns are sold by Leatherworking supply vendors like Worb Strongstitch in Ironforge and Gigget Zipcoil in Orgrimmar. The wrist and waist recipes are learned directly from profession trainers. The most difficult pieces to obtain are the Chest (Pattern: Nightscape Tunic) and Legs (Pattern: Nightscape Pants), as these patterns are world drops from level 40-55 humanoid mobs. Key reagents for the set include Thick Leather, Shadowcat Hide, and Black Dye.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is acquired via crafting and farming world drops. The entire process of farming the patterns and materials is fully soloable at the current max level. The time required to collect the set depends on the drop rate of the two world drop patterns, which can be very low; purchasing the items or patterns from the Auction House is significantly faster. No tier token system is used for this set. All eight pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), making them available for purchase from other players.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to buy the eight completed armor pieces directly from the Auction House. For players who wish to farm the patterns themselves, running level 40-55 dungeons with high humanoid density, such as Zul'Farrak or Sunken Temple, is the best approach. Alternatively, level a Leatherworker to craft the set, as six of the eight patterns are easily obtainable from vendors and trainers. The required crafting materials are common and can also be farmed or bought from the Auction House for a minimal cost.

166.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is a world drop from the Castle Nathria raid in the Shadowlands expansion. Each piece drops from a specific boss within the instance, and the appearance is the same across all difficulties (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic). The Head piece drops from Shriekwing, and the Shoulder piece drops from Huntsman Altimor. The Chest piece is from Artificer Xy'mox, the Feet are from the Sun King's Salvation encounter, and the Wrist is from the Hungering Destroyer. The remaining pieces are the Hands from Sludgefist, the Waist from the Council of Blood, and the Legs from the final boss, Sire Denathrius.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed on a weekly raid lockout, but LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing for four full clears per character each week. The entire raid is easily soloable by a max-level character. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance for a cosmetic item from each boss, the full 8-piece set can take approximately 3-6 weeks to collect if running multiple difficulties. This set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for any class and are Bind-on-Pickup. All pieces for this set drop exclusively within Castle Nathria.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty. You can supplement this by also queuing for the LFR version via the NPC Ta'elfar in Oribos. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. There are no significant skips to bypass bosses that drop set pieces. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault are not systems that can be used to acquire these items from legacy content.

168.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality Bind on Equip (BoE) world drops, primarily found on humanoid mobs between levels 40-50 in Classic zones. The pieces can be acquired by farming these zones or, more commonly, by purchasing them from the Auction House. No piece drops from a specific boss; they are all random zone drops. The full set consists of Dark Hood (Head), Inscribed Leather Spaulders (Shoulder), Inscribed Leather Breastplate (Chest), Inscribed Leather Pants (Legs), Inscribed Leather Gloves (Hands), Inscribed Leather Boots (Feet), Inscribed Leather Belt (Waist), and Inscribed Leather Bracers (Wrist). High-density dungeons such as Zul'Farrak or Maraudon are effective farming locations.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming these pieces in the open world, though dungeons are subject to the 10 instances per hour per account limit. The entire set is easily soloed by any max-level character. Due to the extremely low drop chance and large loot tables of the relevant mobs, targeted farming can take hundreds of runs or many hours with no guarantee of success. This set is for Leather-wearing classes only: Rogue, Druid, Monk, and Demon Hunter. No tier token system is involved, and all pieces are obtained from the same random drop pool.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to purchase the pieces directly from the Auction House. Create a shopping list in the Auction House UI and check periodically for deals, as prices can vary significantly. If you choose to farm the set yourself, use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk in a dungeon with high mob density like Zul'Farrak for the fastest clears. Since all items are BoE, you can farm them on any character and mail them to your intended Leather-wearer or sell duplicates on the Auction House.

169.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from pieces dropped in the level 60, 20-player raid Zul'Gurub, located in the northeast corner of Stranglethorn Vale. The main set pieces are obtained by turning in quest items dropped from specific bosses to Vinchaxa on Yojamba Isle. The Head slot is from the Primal Hakkari Kossack, a quest item from Jin'do the Hexxer. The Shoulder slot is from the Primal Hakkari Shawl from High Priestess Mar'li. The Chest slot is from the Primal Hakkari Tunic from Bloodlord Mandokir. The Legs slot is from the Primal Hakkari Leggings from High Priestess Arlokk. The Feet, Bloodtinged Boots, drop from High Priest Venoxis, while the Waist (Voodoo-woven Belt) and Hands (Grips of the Hidden Temple) are zone drops from trash mobs. The Wrist piece requires Exalted reputation with the Zandalar Tribe.

### Farming Logistics
The original Zul'Gurub instance has a weekly raid lockout, resetting with the weekly maintenance. The entire instance can be easily soloed by any class at current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance for the boss items, collecting the four main token pieces will likely take 4-6 runs. The token-like quest items are class-specific and cannot be used by other classes; for example, the "Zandalar Madcap's" set is for Rogues, while Druids use the "Zandalar Haruspex's" set from the same tokens. Note that the wrist piece is a quest reward tied to Zandalar Tribe reputation, and the waist and hands are random drops from non-boss enemies within the raid. This raid only exists on one difficulty, so there are no LFR/Normal/Heroic/Mythic versions to farm for this specific appearance.

### Farming Tips
